title: Electrical Engineer
description: **The Opportunity** **THE OPPORTUNITY** At Stantec Ireland, you’ll join a growing Energy team at the forefront of a transforming transmission and distribution sector. With rising demand and ambitious decarbonisation targets across Ireland , this is an opportunity to contribute to critical infrastructure shaping the energy transition. As an Electrical Engineer specialising in HV cable design, you’ll work on a diverse range of projects including HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind, substations, and grid connections—supporting initiatives aligned with EirGrid ’s network development. You’ll collaborate within a multidisciplinary team across all project stages, from FEED to delivery, contributing technical expertise while developing your career in a supportive, innovation\-driven environment. **About The Role** ou will take on a key role within our growing Energy team, supporting the design and delivery of high\-voltage cable systems across a range of major infrastructure and renewable energy projects throughout Ireland . Working as part of a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment, you will contribute to projects spanning HVDC interconnectors, offshore and onshore wind farms, substations, and grid connection schemes. Your responsibilities will include supporting and leading design activities across all project phases—from feasibility and FEED through to detailed design and delivery—ensuring high\-quality, efficient, and innovative engineering solutions. You will work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders, including key clients such as EirGrid , helping to address complex technical challenges and deliver infrastructure that underpins Ireland’s transition to a more sustainable energy future. This role offers the opportunity to apply your technical expertise, mentor junior engineers, and play an active part in shaping impactful projects, while continuing to develop your career within a supportive and forward\-looking organisation. **YOUR IMPACT** In this role, you will help deliver safe, reliable and sustainable water infrastructure for communities across Ireland. Through your expertise in process design, you will develop effective treatment solutions that improve water quality and support resilient systems for the future. Working closely with multidisciplinary teams and clients, you will contribute to innovative, practical solutions across the full project lifecycle. Your work will help ensure designs are efficient, compliant and deliver long\-term value while supporting the collaborative culture that defines our team at Stantec. **About You** You are a proactive and technically capable Electrical Engineer with a strong understanding of the electricity transmission and distribution sector. You thrive in collaborative, multidisciplinary teams and are confident coaching and supporting colleagues across the wider team. You will ideally hold a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical Engineering (or a related field) from an accredited institution, with membership of Institution of Civil Engineers (EI) is preferred. You bring experience in civil substation engineering and a broad awareness of HV civil engineering projects. You have hands\-on experience in the design of transmission and distribution systems across all stages (Concept, FEED, and Detail Design), applying international standards for utility clients. You can coordinate effectively across disciplines, resolving design issues and ensuring practical, innovative solutions. **Your Technical Skills Include** * Cable installation design, including routing layouts, trench/trenchless sections, bonding and phasing, pulling, termination, and jointing arrangements. * Proficiency with CYMCAP software for cable sizing calculations to international standards. * Strong computer literacy in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook) and design tools such as AutoCAD or MicroStation. You are adaptable and capable of managing challenging workloads under changing priorities, delivering high\-quality results in a dynamic environment. **WHY JOIN US** * We put people first: We're proud of our friendly, inclusive and collaborative environment where everyone feels valued and driven to succeed. * We are driven to achieve: Named in the top 50 companies to work by Glassdoor 2025\. Recognised by Corporate Knights as one of the world's top 10 most sustainable companies 2025\. \= * We do what is right: Industry leading training and development as well as paid for professional subscriptions. A professional progression plan, supporting you to become chartered. * We are better together: A supportive and collaborative team environment to assist with the growth of your career. * Growth: We're on an exciting growth journey across the Ireland \- we want you to be part of it! * Great Benefits: Competitive salary, enhanced pension plan, annual leave with the option to buy/sell additional days, private medical insurance, group income protection \& life assurance included as standard! **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Electrical Quantity Surveyor | Dublin
description: * Permanent position available with a considerable portfolio of works for the next 5\+ years * To build your career by assisting in the delivery of ‘leading edge’ engineering projects. * To work with a vibrant, agile and multi\-functional team in delivering projects on time, safely, to budget and to the required quality standards. * To benefit from Kirby’s Career Development Programmes that will enhance your leadership capability. * To work with the best. * Excellent package on offer with room for negotiations **About Kirby Group Engineering** Kirby Group Engineering is an international multi\-disciplinary engineering services contractor and leading provider of high\-value engineering services to a number of ‘blue chip’ companies. Operating across Ireland, the UK, Europe and South Africa, Kirby has become the engineering service provider of choice in growth segments such as pharmaceuticals, data centres and power transmission and distribution. Our culture is innovative, collaborative and performance focused. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to grow and develop in a company committed to developing talent and rewarding performance. **Electrical Quantity Surveyor** If you’re an Electrical Quantity Surveyor who enjoys being involved from day one and still standing at the finish line, this role will suit you. You’ll take ownership of projects from tender through to final account, and you’ll have the commercial influence to actually make a dent in profitability — not just report on it afterwards. This isn’t a role where you sit in the background issuing spreadsheets into the void. You’ll be partnering closely with Project Managers, Engineers, subcontractors and clients, making sure the commercial heartbeat of each project stays healthy and predictable (or at least explainable). **What You’ll Be Doing (the Short Version)** * Building and managing BOQs, budgets, reports and profit plans without needing someone hovering over you. * Running interim commercial meetings with clients and internal teams — and being the person who knows exactly where the project stands commercially. * Managing subcontractor accounts, valuations and final accounts, ensuring payments follow agreed terms and retentions don’t fall through the cracks. * Issuing accurate monthly applications, including remeasures, dayworks, changes and any contract claims worth fighting for. * Spotting commercial risks early, raising them quickly and helping to keep surprises to a minimum. * Working closely with Planning Engineers to keep progress reporting aligned with reality rather than optimism. * Costing and agreeing daily work changes, including remeasurement of subcontractor work and site variations. * Keeping meticulous records — because commercial claims don’t win themselves. * Negotiating with clients or their representatives when needed. * Setting a standard of enthusiasm, accuracy and reliability that others can follow. * Keeping information secure and complying with the company’s Information Security Management System. **Who You Probably Are** * Someone who combines accuracy with speed — not one or the other. * Someone who can prioritise their own workload without waiting to be told what’s urgent. * Someone who knows the commercial implications of scheduling decisions and isn’t shy about saying so. * Someone who understands that good communication prevents expensive misunderstandings. **Why this role matters:** The Electrical QS has more influence on project outcomes than most people realise. When you do your job well, the company delivers better margins, fewer disputes and cleaner final accounts. When you don’t, things get messy. We’re hiring someone who takes pride in keeping things un‑messy. *For more information on this role, or other Construction / Engineering opportunities across Ireland, the UK, Europe, or South Africa please review our careers page (Vacancies – Kirby Group Careers) or contact the Talent Acquisition Team at Kirby Group in confidence on \+353 (0\)1 454 0411\.* *Kirby Group Engineering is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, regardless of race, gender, disability, religion, sexual orientation, or age.*

title: Electrical Technician
description: JLL supports the Whole You, personally and professionally. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology to our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people in our industry; and we support them through professional growth, flexibility, and personalized benefits to manage life in and outside of work. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades, and technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, we empower you to shape a brighter way forward so you can thrive professionally and personally. Electrical TechnicianRole Overview We are seeking a qualified Electrical Technician to join our facilities management team in Ireland. The successful candidate will be responsible for the installation, maintenance, repair, and testing of electrical systems and equipment across commercial properties, ensuring compliance with Irish electrical regulations and safety standards. Key Responsibilities The Electrical Technician will carry out planned preventative maintenance and reactive repairs on electrical systems including lighting, power distribution, emergency lighting, fire alarms, and building management systems. You will respond to electrical faults and breakdowns, diagnose problems efficiently, and implement effective solutions to minimize downtime. Conducting electrical safety testing and inspections in accordance with ETCI National Rules for Electrical Installations and maintaining accurate records of all work completed form essential parts of the role. You will ensure all electrical work complies with relevant Irish legislation, health and safety requirements, and building codes. Collaborating with other trades and contractors on site, you will support project work and minor installations as required. The role includes maintaining electrical equipment inventories, ordering parts and materials, and providing technical guidance to junior staff members when needed. Required Qualifications and ExperienceCandidates must hold a recognized electrical qualification such as Advanced Certificate Craft \- Electrical or equivalent Level 6 qualification on the NFQ. Current Safe Electric Registration (RECI or ECSSA) is essential. A minimum of 3\-5 years' post\-qualification experience in commercial or facilities maintenance environments is required, along with comprehensive knowledge of Irish electrical regulations, ETCI wiring rules, and health and safety legislation. Strong diagnostic and problem\-solving abilities are necessary, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team. Excellent communication skills and customer service orientation are important, as is proficiency in using computerized maintenance management systems Desirable Skills Experience with building management systems (BMS), HVAC controls, and access control systems would be advantageous. Knowledge of energy management systems and sustainability initiatives is beneficial. Previous experience in corporate office environments or large commercial properties is valued, as are relevant health and safety certifications including manual handling and working at heights. What We Offer We provide competitive salary packages commensurate with experience, along with comprehensive benefits including pension schemes, health insurance options, and ongoing professional development opportunities. You will work within a supportive team environment with opportunities for career progression and exposure to diverse commercial real estate projects across Ireland. If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements below. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! Personalized benefits that support personal well\-being and growth: JLL recognizes the impact that the workplace can have on your wellness, so we offer a supportive culture and comprehensive benefits package that prioritizes mental, physical and emotional health. About JLL – We’re JLL—a leading professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We have operations in over 80 countries and a workforce of over 102,000 individuals around the world who help real estate owners, occupiers and investors achieve their business ambitions. As a global Fortune 500 company, we also have an inherent responsibility to drive sustainability and corporate social responsibility. That’s why we’re committed to our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world. We’re using the most advanced technology to create rewarding opportunities, amazing spaces and sustainable real estate solutions for our clients, our people, and our communities. Our core values of teamwork, ethics and excellence are also fundamental to everything we do and we’re honored to be recognized with awards for our success by organizations both globally and locally. Creating a diverse and inclusive culture where we all feel welcomed, valued and empowered to achieve our full potential is important to who we are today and where we’re headed in the future. And we know that unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives help us think bigger, spark innovation and succeed together.

A key focus will be making sure graduates, apprentices, interns, and students have a positive and consistent experience across the business. **Role Duties** *Emerging Talent \& People Team Support* * for emerging talent candidates (graduates, apprentices, interns) and liaise with hiring managers to schedule interviews. * Co\-ordinate the recruitment, contract issuing and onboarding of emerging talent program participants. * Engage with university partners and sponsorships – identify opportunities and support engineering and resourcing teams with planning. * Organise travel and logistics for early careers training and UK visits. * Represent Murphy at school visits, careers fairs and early careers events. * Assist with planning of early careers inductions, training sessions, and cohort\-wide events. * Manage any associated invoicing and admin tasks related to outreach events and school/university engagement. * Maintain awareness of emerging talent programmes, initiatives and industry developments, sharing relevant insights and ideas with the Emerging Talent \& Engineering Advisor. * Organise the Murphy TY/Work Experience Programme *Engineering Support* * Coordinate the Engineering Mentor/Mentee programme – maintain register, track feedback, and ensure compliance. * Work with the people development coordinator to maintain and update the Engineering Skills Matrix and CPD Register every 6 months, analyse trends, and coordinate any follow\-up training. * Attend Engineering Leads Meetings, draft minutes, and manage the Action Register. * Support Engineers Ireland accreditation submissions. * Manage Engineers Ireland membership records. * Plan and coordinate college visits. * Keep Engineering Team SharePoint pages and Training Calendars up to date. * Support reporting and data analysis for engineering training and development activities. **Qualifications \& Skills** * Previous experience in a coordinator or administrative role. * Excellent organisational and communication skills. * Ability to manage multiple priorities and work with various stakeholders. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, Word, Teams). * Interest in early talent development and/or engineering functions. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Full driving license and willingness to travel to different sites and occasionally the UK. **What Murphy Offer In Return** * 27 days holiday, plus bank holidays with the option to buy an additional 2 days holiday each year, holidays increase with length of service * Discretionary annual bonus and annual salary review * Above market rate contributory pension scheme * Life assurance, health screening and enhanced sick pay * Generous Private Healthcare Allowance * Enhanced maternity and paternity pay * Extra 2 days holiday for all employees getting married and a wedding bonus * Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development * Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts, discounted gym memberships, cycle to work scheme etc **About Murphy** Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. title: Employment Counsel
description: **Job Details** Job Description: **Legal Counsel Employment and Labor \- Ireland and Europe** **The Role and Impact** As an Attorney at Intel, you will play a pivotal role in delivering expert legal counsel on employment and labor law matters in a complex, multi\-jurisdictional environment. This role is central to ensuring compliance with Irish and European labor and employment laws, while enabling Intel's manufacturing, research, and commercial operations through practical, business\-oriented, and risk\-based legal advice. You will work closely with senior leadership, HR, Employee Relations, and cross\-functional partners to navigate high\-impact workforce decisions, drive legally sound outcomes, and ensure consistency across jurisdictions. This position is based at Intel's Leixlip campus in Ireland and reports to the Director of International Employment Legal within Intel's integrated regional legal team. **Key Responsibilities** **Ireland Employment Law Counsel** * Serve as the primary employment law advisor for Intel Ireland, supporting manufacturing and RD operations. * Provide guidance on Irish employment law, including: * Working Time and leave regulations. * Equality and anti\-discrimination laws. * Disciplinary and dismissal processes. * Redundancy and collective consultation obligations. * Industrial relations and union engagement. * Ensure all workforce actions are legally compliant, well\-documented, and defensible. **European Employment Law Cross\-Border Advisory** Provide legal support on employment and labor issues across Europe, including: * Multi\-country employee relations matters. * Cross\-border reorganizations and restructurings. * Works council and employee representative engagement. * Harmonization of policies and employment practices. * Act as a central coordinating counsel, working closely with external counsel in key European jurisdictions. * Advise on EU\-derived frameworks and compliance obligations impacting workforce management. **Employee Relations and Investigations** Provide legal oversight on complex and sensitive employee relations matters, including: * Workplace investigations harassment discrimination whistleblowing. * Disciplinary actions and terminations. * Grievances and conflict resolution. * Partner closely with HR ER teams to ensure fair process, consistency, and legal defensibility, particularly in high\-risk cases. **Restructuring Redundancy Workforce Transitions** Lead and advise on Ireland and Europe workforce restructuring initiatives, including: * Individual and collective redundancies. * Site reorganizations and business transformations. * Transfers of undertakings TUPE type scenarios. * Ensure compliance with consultation, notification, and employee protection requirements across jurisdictions. **Litigation and Dispute Management** * Manage employment\-related disputes, including labor court claims, tribunal proceedings, and mediations. * Instruct and manage external counsel to ensure efficient strategy execution and favorable outcomes. * Proactively identify trends and risks to minimize litigation exposure. **Policy Compliance and Training** * Advise on and help develop employment policies aligned with Irish and European legal frameworks. * Support implementation of Intel's Code of Conduct, ethics, and compliance standards across the workforce. * Deliver targeted employment law training to HR and business leaders, with a focus on risk prevention and process discipline. **Business and Operational Partnership** **Partner With Business And Operational Leaders To Support** * Workforce aspects of manufacturing and operational compliance including EHS interfaces. * Employment implications in government programs, incentives, and strategic initiatives. * Provide practical, solution\-oriented advice that aligns legal risk with business priorities. **Stakeholder Engagement and Strategic Contribution** * Act as a trusted advisor to senior leadership in Ireland, contributing to site\-level decision making. * Build strong partnerships across HR, ER, compliance, and regional legal teams. * Contribute to regional legal initiatives, including process improvements, compliance frameworks, and capability building across Europe. **Qualifications** * Law Graduate. * Qualified to practice as a Solicitor in Ireland (Law Society of Ireland) or equivalent EU qualification. * 6\-8\+ years post\-qualification experience, with a strong focus on employment and labor law. **Deep Expertise In** * Irish employment law and employee relations. * European labor law frameworks and cross\-border issues. * Proven experience managing investigations, disciplinary processes, and employment litigation. * Experience supporting multi\-jurisdictional workforce matters across Europe. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience in multinational, manufacturing, or technology environments **Strong Familiarity With** * Works councils and employee representation structures in Europe. * Collective consultation and restructuring processes. * Ability to influence and work effectively with senior leadership and cross\-functional teams. * Fluency in an additional European language. **Job Type** Experienced Hire **Shift** Shift 1 (Ireland) Primary Location: Ireland, Leixlip Additional Locations: **Business Group** Intel’s Corporate Affairs, Policy, Integrity, Trade, and Legal (CAPITL) group offers unique opportunities to work in a variety of areas, including counsel to Intel businesses; technology and intellectual property licensing; patent prosecution; trademarks and brands; litigation, mergers, acquisitions and investing; public policy, legislative and regulatory lobbying; global trade, export, import, and customs; and corporate compliance. **Posting Statement** All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, military and veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, gender, gender expression, gender identity, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by local law, regulation, or ordinance. Position of Trust N/A EU Salary We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. Annual Salary Range for this job which could be performed in Ireland is: €73,600\.00 \- €142,500\.00 The compensation range displayed in this posting reflects the minimum and maximum target compensation for this position. Within this range, individual pay is determined by job related skills, experience, and relevant education, training. As part of our total compensation approach, the final offer may include additional pay components and benefits. Your recruiting team will share the confirmed base salary along with any applicable total compensation elements upon employment offer delivery. **Work Model for this Role** This role will require an on\-site presence. \* Job posting details (such as work model, location or time type) are subject to change.

title: Equity Analyst
description: **At Mako, we believe in the power of collaboration to drive innovation in pursuit of our collective ambition; excellence in trading. Our diverse community is connected through a commitment to being the best we can be with the highest standards of integrity.** Join the equity trading team as an Equity Analyst. You will work directly with experienced senior traders and analysts to help them identify risks and opportunities in equities markets that help deliver sustainable short and long\-term success. The Equity Analyst will work closely with our long\-term equity strategy and our special situations desk to create and manage research that is relevant, accurate and actionable. **What you'll be doing:** * Deep\-dive research across sectors, equities, and thematic ideas — going beyond surface consensus to develop proprietary insight * Building comprehensive investment theses from the ground up, including industry mapping, competitive dynamics, and variant perception * Identifying and sizing key catalysts, risk factors, and inflection points across covered names and themes * Constructing and maintaining detailed financial models, valuation frameworks, and proprietary datasets * Tracking structural trends — technological shifts, regulatory change, macro developments — and translating them into actionable equity views * Continuously refining research processes and finding smarter, more scalable ways to generate edge **What we're looking for:** * Excellent analytical skills with very strong attention to detail * Clear, concise communicator who knows what matters and can cut through noise * Genuine intellectual curiosity about equities * Bachelor's degree or equivalent * Front office or markets\-adjacent experience (US equities a plus, not required) **We are Mako** At Mako, we are welcoming, inclusive and collaborative. We work fast and smart in a supportive and dress\-down environment that allows colleagues to be themselves and achieve great things. We uphold the principles of a flat structure that offers unrivalled engagement with senior leadership and career development opportunities. We have a comprehensive benefits package, including: * Flexible leave and hybrid working policies * Private health and dental insurance * Generous pension scheme * Free access to the Mako gym * Employee wellbeing guidance and support * Opportunity to become involved in the rewarding work of the Mako Foundation **About Mako** Mako is a leading options market maker with a global trading footprint. It has been at the forefront of options market making since 1999, from the open outcry trading pits to screen trading and automated algorithmic execution strategies that are driving the future of the industry. From offices in London, Dublin, Amsterdam, Singapore, Sydney, Brisbane and Chengdu, Mako offers the best\-in\-class liquidity solutions across Equities, Fixed Income, Commodities and FX derivatives markets and prides itself in its entrepreneurial, collaborative and philanthropic culture. If you require any reasonable adjustments or assistance during the recruitment process, please email hr@mako.com and we will arrange this. For further information on the Mako Group please refer to our website: www.mako.com. *Mako does not accept unsolicited CVs or candidate details from recruiters or search firms and will not pay any fees to such firms without a signed agreement.*

title: Facilities Assistant
description: **Job Description** **Exceptional Hospitality Starts with You** Picture yourself brightening someone’s day. When you join our Hotels team, that’s exactly what you’ll do as a Facilities Assistant, you’re not just helping maintain the hotel’s physical safety and functionality – you’re spreading the light and warmth of hospitality by delivering memorable experiences that make the stay for every guest. **Responsibilities** Here's what you'll do during a typical day: * Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas: Support hotel facility maintenance Assist in maintaining the physical functionality and safety of the property, including guest rooms, public spaces, and heart\-of\-house areas * Respond to service requests: Quickly handle guest calls and team member work orders * Support a variety of maintenance tasks: Conduct repairs and maintenance across various areas, including carpentry, plumbing, electrical, HVAC and masonry * Assist with preventive maintenance inspections: Inspect facilities and equipment to identify and address preventive maintenance needs * Document maintenance activities: Record completed inspections and repairs and flag any issues requiring further attention **Qualifications** **What It Takes to Make the Stay** At Hilton, Our Core Values Define What It Takes To Succeed Here And Guide The Qualities We Look For In Every Team Member A passion for spreading the light and warmth of **Hospitality** . Acting with **Integrity** and always doing the right thing. Inspiring others through **Leadership** . A belief that **Teamwork** drives the best outcomes. A sense of **Ownership** and accountability. And a focus on the **Now** , bringing urgency and discipline to every moment, knowing it can make a lasting impact. **About Us** **Join an Award\-Winning Workplace Culture** At Hilton, we don’t just deliver exceptional experiences for our guests—we build an exceptional workplace for the Team Members who make it all possible. As a global leader in hospitality, we’ve welcomed more than 3 billion guests worldwide, all while staying true to our founding vision: to fill the earth with the light and warmth of hospitality. Our award\-winning culture has earned us repeated recognition on the World’s Best Workplaces list by Great Place to Work and Fortune. With our suite of world\-class brands , and a company\-wide commitment to providing the best stay for every guest, we’re setting new standards for the future of travel. Whether you’re starting your career or exploring something new, Hilton supports your journey every step of the way. Come for the job, stay for the career—and help us make every stay a little more magical. Curious about life at Hilton? Explore our Careers Blog to see why we’re more than a great place to stay—we’re a great place to work. **About The Team** **How We’ll Help You Thrive** **Benefits** At Hilton, the hospitality we’re known for doesn’t end with our guests. We proudly invest in our Team Members’ wellbeing, supporting you through all of life’s moments. When you join Hilton, our exceptional care extends to you with unmatched perks and benefits, including\*: * Incredible travel perks – Enjoy 110 nights of deeply discounted travel, with room rates as low as $40 USD/night at our world\-class hotels through our Go Hilton travel program * Own a piece of Hilton – Through our employee stock purchase program (ESPP), you can invest in Hilton shares at a 15% discount to help build your financial future * Paid parental leave – Because family matters. We offer paid leave for eligible Team Members, including partners and adoptive parents * Personalized caregiving support – Access dedicated caregiving concierge services to help navigate caregiving for yourself and your loved ones * Crisis concierge – In times of loss, our Crisis Concierge offers a single, compassionate point of contact for both practical support and emotional care * Mental health resources – Your wellbeing comes first. Through our Care for All hub, we provide resources to help our Team Members to care for themselves, care for members of their team, and care for their loved ones. Eligible Team Members receive free counseling and support through our best\-in\-class Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * Benefits availability may vary depending on Team Member’s location as well as terms and conditions of employment and are subject to the terms and conditions of each specific program/plan.

title: Facilities Coordinator
description: **Job Title** Facilities Coordinator **Job Description Summary** The Facilities Coordinator leads and supports the day\-to\-day operations of Sony Interactive Entertainment’s (SIE) Dublin office, delivering a high\-performing, safe, and seamlessly run workplace environment. This role blends hands\-on facilities coordination with strategic workplace management — owning ticket management, vendor relationships, health and safety compliance, space planning, and employee experience. The ideal candidate is a proactive, detail\-oriented workplace professional who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and is equally comfortable working independently and cross\- functionally. **Job Description** **Core Responsibilities** * Workplace \& Facilities Operations * Lead day\-to\-day facilities operations to ensure a safe, functional, and well\-maintained workplace * Act as the primary workplace contact for SIE employees, fostering an environment * Conduct structured facilities and space readiness walks to proactively identify and resolve deficiencies. * Conduct routine and monthly site inspections to ensure the site meets service quality and operational standards. * Ensure meeting rooms, collaboration spaces, and common meet readiness and presentation standards. * Manage all aspects of health and safety for the Dublin offices, ensuring full compliance with applicable regulations and SIE standards. * Support preventative maintenance program tracking and coordination of vendor services (janitorial, maintenance, specialty trades) * Collaborate with building management, local leadership, and cross\-functional teams to maintain operational efficiency aligned with SIE standards. * Ticket Management * Serve as the primary owner for facilities ticket management at the regional sites. * Monitor incoming requests, assign work orders as appropriate, and ensure timely follow through. * Track open and closed tickets and escalate issues that require additional attention. * Maintain strong service delivery by monitoring SLAs, response times, and completion timelines. * Support reporting on ticket volume, trends, KPIs, and service performance metrics. * Communication and Request Intake * Serve as the primary point of contact for facilities\-related Slack channel communications. * Serve as the primary owner of the facilities email alias, including monitoring, routing, and responding to requests. * Gather and act on employee feedback to implement improvements, sharing insights and larger\-scale opportunities with Workplace leadership. * Maintain clear and professional written communication with employees, vendors, and internal stakeholders. * Act as a go\-to resource for employees, addressing facilities\-related inquiries and requests. * Event \& Workplace Experience Support * Support planning and execution of onsite events and employee programming, including coordination of logistics (catering, A/V, space setup, vendor access). * Ensure event space readiness through setup, walkthroughs, and alignment with Facilities, IT/AV, and vendor partners. * Provide day\-of event support as a visible point of contact, troubleshooting issues to ensure a seamless experience. * Assist with post\-event breakdown, space reset, and continuous improvement based on feedback. * Vendor \& Contract Management * Build and manage strong relationships with external vendors and suppliers, ensuring performance aligns with SLAs in collaboration with the Vendor Enablement team. * Monitor vendor safety performance, conduct regular reviews, and proactively address any issues or gaps. * Provide vendor escorting in accordance with site access and safety procedures. * Support maintenance and renewal of service contracts, including documentation and compliance tracking. * Coordinate service schedules, work status updates, and follow\-up communications with vendors and internal teams. * Financial \& Administrative Support * Create and manage Purchase Orders (POs) in alignment with budget and contract requirements. * Process and track vendor invoices, ensuring accuracy and timely payment. * Partner with Facilities Managers to support monthly accruals, including tracking open POs and outstanding expenses. * Assist in budget tracking, variance reporting, and maintaining accurate financial records. * Support vendor onboarding and ensure compliance with contractual and financial requirements. * Create and maintain clear written documentation related to service requests, work orders, and issue resolution. **Skills And Qualifications** * 5\+ years of experience in facilities management, workplace services, or a closely related field * Experience working with Integrated Facilities Management (IFM) contractors and suppliers and monitoring service standards; solid understanding of compliance requirements. * Strong work ethic with the ability to take initiative and function in a fast\-paced working environment with immediate and changing deadlines. * Strong problem solving and decision\-making skills; strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and time management are a must. * Demonstrated strong and effective verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills; ability to interact comfortably and effectively with managers and employees at all levels within the organization. * Proficiency in facilities management platforms, ticketing systems, and Microsoft Office / Google Workspace tools; comfortable using Slack as a primary communication channel. **KPIs / Success Measures** * Ticket response time and SLA compliance * Timely resolution of facilities and workplace requests * Email alias and Slack channel responsiveness * Vendor performance outcomes and contract compliance * Quality of stakeholder communication and employee satisfaction with workplace services * Accuracy and timeliness of financial administration, including POs, invoices, and accruals * Successful delivery of space planning projects, MAC activities, and workplace enhancements * Health and safety compliance and audit readiness INCO: “Cushman \& Wakefield”

title: Fiduciary Monitoring Analyst
description: **Job Description:** * Work as part of the Fiduciary Monitoring Unit team acting as a primary contact in the delivery of an excellent Depositary service to our clients daily. * Work with Clients Trustee operations acting as service provider for the NAV review process, investment restriction monitoring, daily banking and other processes in accordance with the ICSA and approved procedures. * To ensure fiduciary duties are appropriately discharged and the liability for funds domiciled in Ireland is understood and contained. * To ensure a consistent EMEA service and product offering is delivered and where required developed for existing and potential fund clients. * The position exists to protect interests of investors in funds and to ensure franchise and reputation is safeguarded with the appropriate regulatory bodies, mutual funds market and client base **Fiduciary Oversight** * Execution of investment and borrowing monitoring checks for designated funds, monitoring all relevant aspects of investment management activity, including securities lending, OTC / exchange traded derivatives and third party held assets, to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements. * Completion of the cash flow monitoring and tracking of all issues including communication of these to clients. * Assist with the timely and accurate set\-up of new funds from the standpoint of investment monitoring, NAV review, cashflow monitoring and asset register. * Assist in effective oversight of Trustee Operations COE Warsaw. * Interaction with clients in respect of breaches. * Manage the flow of data received by/from internal support teams to ensure that relevant and timely information is provided to clients and their associated service providers. Work with internal support teams and external parties to resolve any outstanding client issues. **Knowledge/Experience:** * 3\+ years Fiduciary or other compliance or financial services related experience. * Experience working with exceptions\-based monitoring systems in Trustee/Depositary, custody, fund accounting or reconciliation systems would be an advantage e.g. CRIMS. * Good knowledge of Trustee / Depositary standards and regulatory responsibilities. * Technical knowledge of financial instruments and investment strategies. * Knowledge of UCITS \& AIFMD investment restrictions would be an advantage. **Skills:** * Teamwork, innovation/creativity, adaptability \& flexibility, positive approach to new ideas and concepts. * Strong knowledge of all MS Office Suite of applications (Word, Excel PowerPoint). * Some experience interacting with MS Excel tools (including VBA and SQL) would be highly desirable **Qualifications:** * Relevant Funds industry qualifications

title: FinOps Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who We Are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What We Do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our People** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose** The FinOps Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education \& Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **Your new company** This organisation is a well\-established, growing professional services firm with a strong presence in Ireland and an expanding international footprint. With a collaborative and design\-led culture, the business delivers complex, high\-profile projects across multiple sectors. You will join a dynamic environment that values innovation, commercial insight, and continuous improvement. **Your new role** As a Finance Business Partner, your job will be to act as a strategic advisor to senior leadership, supporting key decision\-making across a portfolio of projects. You will work closely with directors and operational teams to improve financial performance, provide commercial insight, and ensure strong financial governance. Responsibilities will include: * Partnering with senior stakeholders to provide financial analysis and insights on project performance * Monitoring budgets, costs, profitability, and identifying risks and opportunities * Supporting pricing, bids, and commercial proposals through detailed modelling * Delivering forecasting, reporting, and variance analysis * Managing working capital, including billing and WIP * Contributing to financial planning, resource allocation, and continuous improvement initiatives **What you'll need to succeed** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA or equivalent) * Strong experience in a Finance Business Partnering or commercial finance role * A background in a project\-based or professional services environment is highly beneficial. * Strong financial modelling, analysis, and Excel skills * Ability to influence and communicate effectively with non\-financial stakeholders * Commercial mindset with a proactive, solutions\-focused approach **What you'll get in return** * Competitive salary with performance\-related bonus * Hybrid and flexible working arrangements * Pension contribution and comprehensive benefits package * Opportunities for professional development and further study support * Exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision\-making * A collaborative and innovative working environment **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. title: Financial Accountant
description: **Newly Qualified Accountant – Financial Analyst** This top\-tier global law firm in Dublin 2 is currently recruiting a **Newly Qualified Accountant (ACA / ACCA)** for a Financial Analyst position. As a key member of the Finance team, reporting to the Senior Finance Manager, you will play an important role in enhancing financial control, reporting accuracy, and business insight. This role is ideally suited to a **recently qualified accountant (0–2 years PQE)** with an interest in finance, analytics, and the intersection of finance and IT systems. This is a highly collaborative and cross\-functional role, offering exposure across the wider business and direct engagement with senior stakeholders. **Key Responsibilities** * Drive and maintain monthly management reporting, including analysis against operational KPIs at both departmental and individual fee earner level * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support business decision\-making and performance improvement * Collaborate with the Finance Management Team on presentations, reporting packs, and board\-level reporting * Liaise with Partners and internal stakeholders to respond to finance\-related queries * Support and mentor trainees and junior team members where required * Lead or contribute to finance transformation projects and continuous improvement initiatives * Assist with ad hoc analysis, reporting, and business insights as required * Present KPI performance at individual fee earner level * Act as a key liaison with the internal IT team in relation to the practice management system * Support system enhancements, implementations, and process improvements **About You** * Recently qualified ACA / ACCA (0–2 years post\-qualification experience) * Strong academic background and technical accounting knowledge * Excellent analytical, reconciliation, and problem\-solving skills * Strong attention to detail with advanced IT and Excel capabilities * Exposure to systems, reporting tools, or implementations is an advantage * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Able to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and able to manage multiple priorities effectively **Why Join Us** * Excellent salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model * Strong exposure to senior stakeholders in a leading international law firm * Excellent scope for career development and internal progression **How to Apply** To apply, please contact Richard Minchin at Lex Consultancy.

title: Financial Controller
description: **Your new company** This is an established, financially strong, Dublin\-based business operating in a highly specialised and fast\-growing sector. The organisation is a recognised market leader with a strong international footprint, exporting the majority of its products globally. With cutting\-edge technology, high barriers to entry, and significant growth momentum, the company offers a unique opportunity to join a high\-performing leadership team and contribute to its continued expansion. **Your new role** As Group Financial Controller, you will play a key role within the senior management team, partnering closely with the CEO and COO. You will take ownership of the financial operations across the group, ensuring robust reporting, strong controls, and insightful analysis to support strategic decision\-making. Key responsibilities include: * Leading the preparation of group management accounts, including variance analysis, balance sheet reviews, and forecasting * Overseeing budgeting, financial planning, and cash flow management * Ensuring accuracy of costing and inventory valuation processes * Managing AP, AR, and payroll functions * Maintaining strong financial controls, policies, and compliance frameworks (including multi\-jurisdiction VAT and duty) * Leading internal audit activity and reporting findings to senior leadership * Partnering with operational teams to support business performance * Supporting audit, tax, and external advisor relationships * Contributing to strategic initiatives, financial modelling, and potential acquisitions This role offers a clear progression path to Finance Director, with the potential for broader leadership exposure over time. **What you'll need to succeed** * Fully qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA) with a relevant degree * Minimum 10\+ years' experience in a similar finance leadership role * Strong technical expertise across financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and costing * Experience in an SME or growth environment is an advantage. * Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and support strategic decisions * Excellent analytical skills and high attention to detail * Advanced Excel skills and experience with ERP/financial systems * A hands\-on, proactive approach with the ability to operate in a fast\-paced environment **What you'll get in return** * Competitive salary in the region of €100,000 \- €120,000 * Performance\-related bonus * Pension contribution * Clear and structured career progression pathway to Finance Director * Opportunity to work closely with executive leadership and shape business strategy * Exposure to international operations in a high\-growth sector **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. title: Financial Crime Associate
description: Financial Crime Associate Dublin, Ireland **About The Role** A leading financial services organisation is seeking an Associate to join its Transaction Control Sanctions Screening Team. This is an excellent opportunity for a detail oriented professional to work in a high volume, fast paced environment supporting critical compliance and financial crime prevention processes across the EMEA region. **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct sanctions screening on live transactions and customer records across the EMEA region. * Process a high volume of transactions in real time, with daily throughput expectations exceeding 400 transactions. * Review Politically Exposed Person (PEP) alerts within the firm’s sanctions screening systems. * Utilise industry standard tools to analyse and assess screening results. * Escalate potential matches or concerns in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements. * Maintain high levels of accuracy and attention to detail in a time\-sensitive environment. **Skills \& Experience** * Prior experience in sanctions screening, AML, or financial crime compliance is desirable. * Familiarity with screening tools is advantageous. * Strong analytical skills and attention to detail. * Ability to manage high volume workloads and meet deadlines. * Good understanding of PEP, sanctions, and regulatory frameworks. * Excellent organisational and communication skills. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Fitness Coach for ladies
description: The Curves Coach reports to the owner/manager and is responsible for helping members build a healthy lifestyle;guiding members toward their goals, supporting them each step of the way and celebrating every victory. Coaches are expected to meet all performance expectations, service standards, and operational procedures asset out and documented during Training or by the Owner/Manager. Service is the cornerstone of Curves. A Coach’s primary responsibility is on the circuit, coaching members to reach their fitness, health or weight\-loss goals. They inspire member to change their lives through positive reinforcement of goals, promoting changes in habits and healthy choices and genuinely celebrating success. A coach will create opportunities for members to share their Curves experience with friends and family. There is also the opportunity to sell memberships and a full bonus/incentive plan is in place. No experience necessary as full comprehensive training will be provided. Job Type: Part\-time Pay: From €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * Gym membership * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: General Dentist
description: Principal Associate Dentist **Full\-time \| Flexible Sessions Available** Earn €125,000–€230,000\+ per year while focusing on dentistry, not administration. We are an established, 100% Irish\-owned dental group with more than 40 years of experience and a growing network of clinics across Dublin and the East of Ireland. We are seeking an ambitious **Principal Associate Dentist** to join our established and growing Irish dental group. This is an opportunity to join a modern, patient\-focused group where the operational burden is handled for you, allowing you to concentrate on delivering excellent clinical care and developing your career. What You'll Receive * On\-target earnings from **€125,000 in Year 1**, with potential to exceed **€230,000 per year** as your patient list develops * Direct mentorship and support from our Clinical Director * Senior clinical leadership opportunities * Flexible sessional working * Funded CPD and group development programmes * State\-of\-the\-art clinics featuring modern equipment including digital scanning and advanced technologies * Dedicated operational and administrative support teams * Marketing support to help maintain a busy diary and strong patient flow * Professionally trained reception and treatment coordinator teams to support patient conversion and care delivery What We Handle * Accounts and administration * Bookkeeping and payroll * Compliance and paperwork * Group purchasing and supplier negotiations * Marketing and patient acquisition * Operational management and clinic support You focus on clinical excellence.....we take care of the rest. About You * Registered with, or eligible for registration with, the Dental Council of Ireland * An experienced dentist looking to progress into a senior clinical position * Committed to high\-quality patient care and continuous professional development * Interested in contributing to the growth and leadership of a modern dental group Apply Today If you're looking for a senior clinical role with excellent earning potential, modern facilities, and the support of an established Irish dental group, we'd love to hear from you. **Apply with your CV and a brief note outlining your interest in the Principal Associate opportunity.** Job Types: Full\-time, Part\-time Experience: * General Dentistry: 3 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * IDC registration (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: General Operative
description: Production **Job Purpose** To support the business in achieving its daily targets **Reports To** Shift Supervisor **Key Responsibilities** Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to: * Meet daily production targets on output, waste, yield, efficiency, start\-up time by working as part of a team * Accurately record all production data on the relevant sheets provided at set time frequencies (such as downtime, waste, process sheets, non\-conformances etc.) * Ensure that all quality tests are completed as per protocols and all traceability details are carried out in line with systems requirements * Control and responsibility of all items within the area such as production tools, probes, machine parts in line with metal procedures and other such foreign matter procedures in place. Replacing when required and reporting of items if misplaced * Carry out all tasks to ensure that food safety requirements are achieved * Maintain the work area and machines in a tidy / safe / hygienic manner * Operate all equipment in accordance with training provided and approved SOP * Follow all hygiene and safety procedures * Offer suggestions / new initiatives to a changing / developing process * Work within the quality standards as set out by the business * Ensure all products are produced and packed to company / customer specification and on the ‘get it right first time’ motto * Inform line manager of any maintenance issues identified * Communicate with other members of staff to ensure continuous supply of materials to the production line to ensure uninterrupted stoppages to the process * Be flexible to ensure all orders are met, e.g., in the event of a breakdown occurring on the line **Skills / Qualifications / Experience** * Good interpersonal and communication skills * Excellent understanding of food safety (HACCP) and Health \& Safety processes * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * At least 6 months in a FMCG environment * Ability to work in a team environment * Shows flexibility in all aspects of the role * Understands the business and what needs to get done to help the business to be successful * Is proactive and thinks ahead * Excellent written and verbal English Note: As with all positions, due to the dynamic nature of Arrow Group business, key responsibilities will evolve and change over time. Some travel will be required. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate’s eligibility to work in Ireland Apply Now

title: General Operative - Nightshift
description: * Pack out \& lining orders, bulk retrieval, order processing, fridge \& general assembly, order scanning, putting away stock, completion of van sheets –In line with Uniphar Standard operating procedures. * Unload pallets from Dublin truck using forklift, confirm quantities \& line in run sequence. * Work on the IWACS system * Work with the drivers EPOD system and have working knowledge of same. * Unload pallets for goods inwards when required. * Liaise with Citywest warehouse if required. * Process empty totes / lids for return to Citywest. * Record any issues in the hand over book for shift supervisor/manager * Work with the Supervisor and Line Leader to process Bulk Stock and/or assemble customer orders in an efficient and accurate manner **Key Skills \& Experiences** * Can do attitude. * Very good verbal and written communication skills. * Ability to work on own initiative and as part of a team. * High level of attention to detail. * Strong Customer Focus. * Respect, collaborative approach. * Highlight operational improvements to enhance service and productivity. * Basic Computer skills **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: General Operative / B Driver - Traffic Management (Blanchardstown)
description: Interested in a career in traffic management? If you believe you have the skills and experience required for this role, please review the details below and click the **Apply button** to apply. **Traffic Management Operative \- B driver** GKTM is a traffic management company providing traffic management solutions nationwide, having operated at the forefront of the industry over the past decade, working on many large infrastructure and transport projects. **Role** We currently have a number of vacancies for Traffic Management Operatives B driver to be based primarily in our Blanchardstown Depot. As a **Traffic Management Operative \- Driver**, you will play a vital role in supporting the safe and efficient movement of traffic around roadworks, maintenance projects, and special events. Your primary responsibility will be driving company vehicles to transport traffic management equipment, materials, and personnel to and from work sites. You will also assist with the safe loading, unloading, installation, maintenance, and removal of traffic control measures, including signs, cones, barriers, and temporary traffic management systems. You will ensure all work is carried out in accordance with company procedures, traffic management plans, and health and safety regulations. The role requires a strong commitment to safety, excellent driving skills, reliability, and the ability to work effectively as part of a team. You will also interact professionally with clients, contractors, and members of the public while representing the company on site and on the road. **Training** will be delivered upon commencement to ensure the successful candidate understands how to carry out their duties safely while on site. The successful candidate will look forward to the following: **Benefits** * Competitive rates of pay * Opportunities to progress within company * Company organised events * In depth traffic management training, both in\-house and externally * Full\-time, permanent positions are available * Overtime available **Requirements** * Valid Safe Pass * Manual Handling * Full B driver license * Own transport essential for shifts which may begin outside of the hours of public transport **Responsibilities:** * **Traffic Management Equipment Setup:** Safely install and set up traffic management equipment, signs, cones, barriers, and other equipment according to established plans and procedures under the guidance of the site supervisor. * **Traffic Management:** Monitor traffic flow and respond to changing conditions, adjusting traffic control measures as necessary to ensure the safety of both workers and the public. * **Driving Company Vehicles:** Operate company vehicles, such as trucks or vans, to transport traffic management equipment and personnel to and from work sites. Drive vehicles with caution, obeying all traffic laws and company policies, and ensure the safe loading and securing of equipment. * **Safety Compliance:** Strictly adhere to all safety regulations, policies, and guidelines while carrying out duties, including wearing appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). * **Equipment Maintenance:** Perform routine checks and maintenance of traffic management equipment to ensure proper functionality, reporting any issues to the supervisor. * **Communication:** Effectively communicate with colleagues, supervisors, and the public, providing clear and courteous instructions and information regarding traffic control measures and diversions. * **Emergency Response:** Act promptly and effectively in case of emergencies or incidents, following established procedures to mitigate risks and ensure public safety. * **Documentation:** Maintain accurate records and documentation related to traffic management activities, including equipment usage and maintenance reports. * **Environmental Awareness:** Consider and implement environmentally friendly practices in traffic management, including waste management and pollution control. * **Continuous Improvement:** Stay updated on industry best practices and participate in training programs to improve skills and knowledge related to traffic management. **Profile of the ideal candidate:** * Previous experience in traffic management or a related field is preferred but not essential. * Flexible, willing to work shifts during the day or night in line with the requirements of our clients. * Punctual. * Willing to work down the country for up to five consecutive days. * React positively to feedback provided by your mentor and other leaders within the organisation. * Strong attention to detail and the ability to work efficiently and safely in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication and teamwork skills. * Physical fitness and the ability to lift heavy equipment and work outdoors in various weather conditions. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €14\.50 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Experience: * General Operative: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * own transport for early commute (required) * Manual Handling (required) * Safe Pass (required) * Full B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Grade V - ICT Cyber Security Support Officer
description: he Child and Family Agency was established on 1st January 2014 and is responsible for a range of statutory functions including provision of child protection, alternative care, specified regulatory services and a range of family support services. The Agency has commenced a major improvement programme with significant focus on Practice, Culture and Structure. The Agency currently has responsibility for a budget of circa €1\.2billion and delivers its services through over 5,500 people in 259 locations across the Country. The Child and Family Agency has responsibility for the following range of services: * Child Protection and Welfare * Parenting, Family Support and Early Help Services * Alternative Care * Birth Information \& Tracing and Adoption * Tusla Education Support Services (TESS) * Children’s Service Regulation * Counselling and Therapeutic Supports Further information is available on www.tusla.ie **About Tusla ICT** Tusla ICT provides the innovation, systems, and support to assist the Agency in delivering its services to children and families, as well as achieving its business and strategic objectives. title: Graduate Business Development Representative (SaaS Sales)
description: We're looking for a curious, ambitious and motivated **Graduate Business Development Representative** to join our Growth team at Alkimii. Looking to kick\-start your career in SaaS sales? We're looking for a curious, ambitious and motivated Graduate Business Development Representative to join our Growth team at Alkimii. You'll learn how modern B2B SaaS sales works from the ground up, working alongside experienced Business Development Executives and Account Executives while building the skills needed to grow into a future commercial leader. **What is Alkimii?** At Alkimii, we're on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hospitality teams streamline workflows, improve communication and make better operational decisions — all in one place. We're a fast\-growing SaaS company, and our Growth team plays a critical role in how we scale. **The Opportunity** This role is the first step on a structured commercial career path. Over your first year, you'll learn: * Modern SaaS prospecting * Outbound sales * Customer qualification * HubSpot CRM * Hospitality technology * Commercial communication * Pipeline generation You'll work closely with experienced Business Development Executives, Account Executives and Marketing professionals who will help accelerate your development. For high performers, this role provides a clear progression path into a **Business Development Executive (BDE)** position. **What You'll Be Doing** **Prospecting \& Research** • Research hotel groups and hospitality businesses across the UK and Ireland • Identify key decision makers within target accounts • Build prospect lists using LinkedIn, HubSpot and modern prospecting tools • Maintain accurate and structured prospect data **Outreach \& Opportunity Creation** • Use modern sales and AI\-powered prospecting tools to identify opportunities and improve productivity • Engage prospects through phone calls, email and LinkedIn • Introduce Alkimii to potential customers • Generate interest and book qualified meetings • Support outbound campaigns alongside Marketing • Learn and apply best practices in SaaS prospecting **CRM \& Pipeline Management** • Maintain accurate records in HubSpot • Track prospect engagement and activity • Log calls, emails and meetings consistently • Ensure CRM data remains accurate and up to date **Learning \& Development** • Complete Alkimii Academy certification • Shadow Business Development Executives and Account Executives • Participate in call reviews and coaching sessions • Learn objection handling and discovery techniques • Build a strong understanding of our product and industry **What Success Looks Like** **In your first 3 months** •
