title: Graduate Energy Consultant
description: We are currently seeking applications for trainee energy consultants. This would suit any graduate in the built environment in fields such as : * Building Surveying, * Architectural Technology, * Mechanical Engineering, * Construction Management, * Building Services Engineering etc. All training will be provided and paid for. The candidate will start off as a domestic BER assessor in an environment where the necessary experience will be gained to develop into an experienced energy consultant. This could be an exciting new career as a professional in a rapidly growing industry. The role of an energy consultant typically involves three days in the office and two days surveying properties. The successful candidate will be focused on providing technical specifications and advice for customers, most of whom are contractors carrying out energy retrofit projects. Our office is based in Tullyallen Village, Co. Louth. In addition we do not operate on a rigid schedule for office days and use flexible working hours i.e. start early and early finish or vice versa. There is full use of a company vehicle at all times and so no commuting costs. **Requirements:** * The entry requirements to become a registered BER assessor are either: * A) Minimum QQI Level 6 in a construction related qualification, * B) Minimum QQI Level 6 in a non construction related qualification, but with at least two years relevant construction experience, * Ideally candidates would have a qualification in building surveying, construction studies, engineering, architectural technology, project management, civil engineering or similar, however, this is not a requirement and all candidates will be considered, * Full clean driving license. **Essential Skills**: * Possess good communication skills, * Customer focused, * Ability to work on your own initiative The successful candidate will have the chance to work alongside a team of experienced retrofit professionals \& gain knowledge of domestic and commercial energy ratings, building regulations, air tightness, ventilation validation and energy retrofits of dwellings. A generous package is available for the right candidate. For more information see below links to our websites: www.ventilationvalidation.ie www.necber.ie Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €31,200\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Licence/Certification: * full driving licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Engineer - Engines-EN
description: Do You Want To Progress Your Career With The Global Leader In Aviation? Under The Guidance Of Senior Engineers, Our Graduate Engineers Will Be Given The Opportunity To Gain Hands\-on Experience Across Our Technical Support Services Department Which Includes The Engine Management Technical Support Services Department specializes in managing powerplants for our clients, including engine phase in and phase out, shop visit oversight, monitoring engine condition, records review and conducting maintenance forecasts. As a Powerplant Graduate Engineer, you'll gain experience providing support to our clients, with involvement in a large variance of projects ensuring that there is never a dull moment for our graduate engineers. On completion of the programme, you’ll be the type of person who has the proven ability to solve problems with sound judgment and have effective communication skills as well as excellent organizational skills and computer skills. As part of the programme, you will be mentored by senior engineers, have the opportunity to rotate across the different engineering services departments and be involved in new and existing projects. All the above will provide you with the best training in the industry, while following a clear and structured development path. **Education Required** Applicants will have recently completed or are in the final year of a Technical Degree (B.Eng, B.Sc etc.) **The Package** We offer a competitive salary as well as a range of benefits. **Next Steps** This is an excellent opportunity to join our Engineering graduate programme and to join a multi\-award\-winning company, which is the global leader in its field. **About CAE** *At CAE, our mission is clear: to help make the world a safer place. For nearly 80 years, we’ve driven innovation in simulation, training, and mission readiness to support critical operations worldwide. By leveraging advanced technologies, we empower our customers to operate smarter, faster, and more sustainably. Join a purpose\-driven organization where bold ideas are encouraged, collaboration drives progress, and your growth fuels our shared success.* **Position Type** Regular **Equal Opportunity \& Accommodations** *CAE is committed to providing equal opportunities to all applicants, regardless of race, nationality, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, disability, neurodiversity, veteran status, age, or other characteristics protected by law. We encourage applicants who may not meet every qualification to apply. Reasonable accommodations are available—contact your recruiter or email* *CAECarrieres\-Careers@cae.com* *if needed.* **Data Privacy** Privacy Statement \| CAE *As part of our process, we may use AI‑supported tools to help review applications, with human decision‑making at every step. CAE thanks all applicants for their interest. However, only those whose background and experience match the requirements of the role will be contacted.*

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included. This is an ideal **first job** , **graduate program alternative** , or **gap\-year career launch** for ambitious, globally minded graduates. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** . Whether you’re a recent graduate, or exploring your first international role, this is your chance to **earn, travel, and grow professionally and personally** . **Apply now and start your international career with English 1\.** \#EntryLevel \#Graduates \#EnglishTeacher \#ESL \#TEFL \#TeachingJobs \#GraduateJobs \#hiring \#jobs \#entrylevel \#teachercns \#graduates \#esl \#tefl \#teach \#JobAlert \#NowHiring \#JobOpening \#CareerOpportunities \#JobSearch \#WorkWithUs \#LinkedInJobs \#JobSeeker \#OpenToWork \#CareerGoals \#JobHunt \#NewJob \#CollegeGraduates \#BachelorsDegree \#RecentGrad \#ClassOf2026 \#JobSearch \#CareerSuccess \#EntryLevelJobs \#HiringNow \#YoungProfessionals \#CareerGrowth \#Networking \#LinkedInNetworking \#FutureLeaders \#UniversityAlumni \#HigherEd \#AlumniNetwork \#CollegeSuccess

title: Graduate Programme - Insurance - September 2026
description: By joining Sedgwick, you'll be part of something truly meaningful. It’s what our 33,000 colleagues do every day for people around the world who are facing the unexpected. We invite you to grow your career with us, experience our caring culture, and enjoy work\-life balance. Here, there’s no limit to what you can achieve. Newsweek Recognizes Sedgwick as America’s Greatest Workplaces National Top Companies Certified as a Great Place to Work® Fortune Best Workplaces in Financial Services \& Insurance Graduate Programme \- Insurance \- September 2026 **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme (Ireland)** **Start your career in casualty \& liability claims with Sedgwick** Are you a recent graduate looking for a career where no two days are the same? Want to build real expertise while helping people through challenging situations? Our **Graduate Loss Adjuster Programme** is designed to give you everything you need to become a confident, skilled casualty or liability adjuster. **What You’ll Get** * Structured training * Learn the fundamentals of loss adjusting, including handling casualty and liability claims * Mentorship from day one * Be paired with an experienced adjuster who will guide and support your development * Professional qualification support * We’ll support you to achieve your APA insurance qualification (January 2027 sitting) * Real experience * Work on live claims and build practical skills from the start **What The Role Involves** As a Graduate Loss Adjuster, you will: * Support the management of casualty and liability claims from start to finish * Work with customers, insurers and third parties to investigate claims * Gather and assess information to understand what happened and what is covered * Help agree fair settlements * Keep accurate records and prepare reports * Build relationships with clients and stakeholders * Learn how to manage your own caseload over time You’ll split your time between **field visits** (homes or business premises) and **office\-based work** , developing both technical and people skills. **Why this role is different** * You’ll help people during difficult situations (e.g. injury claims or liability disputes) * You’ll develop investigation, negotiation and problem\-solving skills * You’ll gain a clear career path in insurance and claims * You’ll be part of a supportive, experienced team **Who We’re Looking For** We’re looking for graduates who are: * Curious and eager to learn * Strong communicators (clear, confident and empathetic) * Organised and detail\-focused * Good problem\-solvers * Interested in building a long\-term career in insurance / claims **Requirements** * Degree in Law, Business, Construction or Criminology * Full clean driving licence (essential for site visits) * Willingness to travel across Ireland when required **Why join Sedgwick** * Career growth – clear progression and development opportunities * Supportive environment – mentorship and structured learning * Meaningful work – make a real difference to people and businesses * Inclusive culture – a workplace that values diversity and fairness Sedgwick is an Equal Opportunity Employer. The statements contained in this document are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by a colleague assigned to this description. They are not intended to constitute a comprehensive list of functions, duties, or local variances. Sedgwick retains the discretion to add or to change the duties of the position at any time.

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Greek Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Greek * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Graduate Sales Programme
description: **Location: Sandyford, Dublin** **Full\-time role** **Salary: €30,000 \+ commission** **The Opportunity** Join an accredited immersive eighteen\-month graduate programme that will give you exposure to work across our various brands in the utilities sector, including PrepayPower and Yuno Energy — leaders in the utility sector in Ireland. The programme provides valuable sales experience, operational exposure and allows you to build professional and personal skills to forge a career in Sales or beyond. **Overview** The programme is designed to prepare you for successful careers in sales through rotations, coaching, and hands\-on experience. The role offers a unique blend of telesales and field sales experience, providing exposure to multiple sales channels and a strong foundation for a long\-term career in sales. After successfully completing the programme, there will be the possibility to progress to a commercial team. **Who Is It For?** * Recent graduates (0–2 years' experience) * Degrees in Business, Marketing, Economics, or any discipline with strong commercial acumen * Desired skills: * + Communication \& persuasion + Resilience \& adaptability + Curiosity \& learning agility + Results\-driven mindset ### ### **Benefits** * **Competitive salary and bonus structure** * **Private Pension Contribution Scheme** with Zurich Life * **Discounted Health Insurance \- Group Scheme** with Laya Healthcare * **Employee Assistance Program** provided by TELUS Health * **Free Online GP Services** via Webdoctor.ie * **Excellent career progression opportunities** in a growing company with strong market presence * **Fast\-paced, friendly team environment** * **Continuous training and development** * **Annual leave that increases with service** * **Exclusive PrepayPower Staff Tariff** * **Modern offices** with excellent facilities in Sandyford * **Active CSR, Wellness, and Social initiatives** plus great social events * **Refer\-a\-Friend Scheme** * **TaxSaver Travel Tickets \& Bike\-to\-Work Scheme** ### **About Yuno Energy** Since entering the Irish market in late 2023, we've been transforming the residential energy sector with a bold mission: to help consumers save energy and money through a data\-driven, app\-first experience. We are redefining how people understand and engage with their energy. Our innovative approach delivers radical transparency and rewards energy efficiency. With just a few taps, customers can gain a clear view of their energy habits—tracking every hour, unit, and cent of their usage. In 2025, together with PrepayPower, we proudly reached over 350,000 customer connections. **Why Work at Yuno Energy** At Yuno Energy, we believe work should be both challenging and rewarding. Ranked among Ireland's Great Places to Work*,* we offer ambitious individuals a dynamic, fast\-paced environment with exciting opportunities for growth. We are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to treating all employees, customers, and stakeholders with dignity and respect. We value diverse talent and are dedicated to building an inclusive workplace that drives innovation and delivers exceptional services. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences. Yuno Energy is part of the Yuno Group, alongside PrepayPower and firmus Energy. With almost 600 employees and growing, we are shaping the future of electricity, gas, and broadband in Ireland. Join us and become part of a dynamic, ambitious team that's shaping the future of energy. **Privacy** By applying for this role, you agree to Yuno Energy processing your personal data for recruitment purposes. We handle all applications in line with our Privacy Notice You can view our Careers Privacy Policy \| Ireland's Newest Electricity Provider \- Yuno Energy for more information.

title: Hiring for 2026! Entry-Level English Teacher (ESL) | No Experience Needed
description: **Launch Your International Career with Purpose and Adventure** Are you searching for a **rewarding, entry\-level job** that lets you **travel the world** , **work abroad** , and gain **real\-world experience** all while making a difference? title: Host Service & Reception (m/f/d)
description: **At Ruby, your personality really matters, your time really matters, and we strongly believe we groove way better** **together. At Ruby, we keep breaking new ground.** Hey Sunshine, You think travelling is more than just the next summer holiday, but the **feeling of being at home anywhere in the world** ? That your workplace should be a place with **personality and soul** , where new ideas come to life? Then you are the missing olive in our Martini, the missing melody in our music, and the **heartbeat of our Ruby Molly** . We are a fast\-growing hospitality group with existing hotels and workspaces in some of **Europe's most exciting locations** and many more projects under construction. We **break new ground** with our **Lean Luxury philosophy** , creating a contemporary and affordable form of luxury. **Join us and make it your own story** **Trust Us, You Won't Get Bored, As You** * are not only an all\-rounder and multitasking talent, but also the first point of contact for our guests to make them feel at home from the very first moment to the last drink * support our guests with the self\-check\-in process and take on additional front desk responsibilities * prepare cocktails, drinks, and delicious snacks with a few skillful moves, ensuring smooth and seamless service * lovingly take care of our breakfast buffet in the morning and wow our guests with your barista skills * roll up your sleeves when it comes to goods receiving, inspection, and storage, always keeping an eye on the perfect mise\-en\-place according to our setups and standards * share your insider tips with our guests, pointing them to the best hotspots in the city **We've Been Waiting For You, Since You Have/are** * ideally gained some experience in the hotel industry, don’t worry if you don’t know what "mise\-en\-place" means yet 😉 * flexible and ready to work in shifts * counting reliability, multitasking and your willingness to learn among your strengths * empathetic with others and love sharing your enthusiasm with those around you * a great sense of humour, enjoy working in a team, and love interacting with our guests * speaking English fluently **What's in for you? That's how we groove:** **Ruby, Ruby, Ruby** * your style, your smile, your ideas – bring your personality to work. Maybe even with a new tattoo, piercing or hair colour on us? * better together – at work, during team events or just because it’s Monday 😉 **'Cause I'm having a good time** * less work, same money – for us, full\-time means 35 hours and every hour counts * no more excuses – we support your sports program * LEGEN...wait for it...DARY staff rates \- for you and your travel buddy **Money, Money, Money** * sip, snack, repeat \- with your Ruby F\&B\-Credit * no matter how you get to work – a mobility allowance comes on top * take it to the next level – secure yourself some extra cash through talent scouting and positive reviews **Ain't no mountain high enough** * level up your game – with trainings, coaching, and mentoring, you get the chance to grow, just like Ruby does * innovation is part of our DNA – Lean Luxury, paperless, cashless, … you name it **Where have you been so long?** Whether you’re a **social butterfly** with a flair for cocktail\-shaking, a creative soul with an eye for design, or a pro at planning and **behind\-the\-scenes magic** \- there’s a place for you at Ruby. We’re always looking for **friendly faces and passionate team players** . Not perfect CVs, but **great personalities** , who’ll help us create hotels and workspaces with **character and soul** . So, whatever makes you tick, join us and start your Ruby story. **Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion** We believe that... * you can love whoever you want to * you should decide for yourself whether and with which pronouns you would like to be addressed * you can be proud of your heritage and culture * you don't have to justify your religion or world view * you are good, just as you are and make our team and Ruby's diversity unique We can't deal with: isms Racism, sexism, heterosexism, antisemitism, and such kind of isms are not tolerated here. If You Want To Know What To Expect, Listen To Carolina's Story From Our Ruby Zoe In London. You Can Find Even More Ruby Stories Here www.ruby\-hotels.com/stories Get to know our People \& Culture Team Any questions about our pitch? Then contact us at jobs@ruby\-hotels.com or \+49 89 125 095 220\. Your Ruby Recruitment Team

title: Housekeeping Assistant Elm Green
description: **An Exciting opportunity for the right candidate.** We are looking for a Domestic Assistant/Cleaner to work in our Eln Green Nursing Home Dunsink Lane Dublin as part of Trinity Care Group a well\-respected, multi\-award\-winning Nursing Home Group, part of the international DomusVi Group. We provide private residential, convalescent and rehabilitative care in our purpose\-built Nursing Homes. We have centres in Dublin, Meath, Louth, Kildare, Kilkenny, and Cavan. **Key Responsibilities:** To ensure the highest standard of cleanliness in all areas at all times. Responsible for carrying out of your duties in the laundry including bed linens etc., and personal items for clients. **Duties** : * To be responsible for the thorough servicing of your allocated area to the defined standard. * To offer excellent service to all clients/visitors and ensure that all queries, complaints and compliments are relayed back to your supervisor. * To maintain the appearance of your trolley, press and working area at all times, restocking your trolley before going off duty, and ensuring presses are secure. * To have an understanding of usage, safety and hazards of all chemicals and cleaning products. * Record keeping. * Ensure that you maintain the cleanliness and good order of your area of assignment and associated areas to the highest possible standard. * Ensure efficient and economical use of materials and equipment. * To adhere to specifications, standards and procedures while at work. * To report mechanical defects and needs for repairs. * To take all necessary steps to ensure the maximum security of your area of assignment and all equipment and supplies contained therein. * To carry our any reasonable requests by management **Skills:** Cleaning, chemicals, Domestic Cleaning **Important Notice For candidates** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Trinity Care emails, with an @trinitycare domain.

title: Hybrid IT Support Engineer (On-Prem & Cloud)
description: **Hybrid IT Support Engineer** We are seeking a highly skilled **Hybrid IT Support Engineer** to lead and support **system upgrades, migrations, and infrastructure enhancements** in a healthcare environment. This role ensures the reliability, security, and efficiency of **critical IT systems**, including **Electronic Health Records (EHR), clinical applications, servers, and cloud environments**. The ideal candidate will have expertise in **healthcare IT infrastructure**, regulatory compliance (**JCI, HIQA**), and IT service management best practices. **Job Title** Hybrid IT Support Engineer **Department** Information Technology (IT) **Reporting To** IT Infrastructure Manager / IT Operations Manager **Location** Hybrid (Hospital Sites and Remote Support) **Job Purpose** The Hybrid IT Support Engineer is responsible for providing technical support across hospital and healthcare environments, ensuring the availability, security, and performance of clinical and non\-clinical IT systems. The role involves supporting healthcare staff, maintaining end\-user devices, troubleshooting infrastructure issues, and assisting with healthcare applications while adhering to data protection and patient confidentiality requirements. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Provide 1st and 2nd line IT support to hospital staff, clinicians, administrative teams, and external partners. * Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, network, printer, and application issues. * Manage incidents and service requests through the IT Service Management (ITSM) platform. * Escalate complex issues to specialist teams where appropriate. * Participate in on\-call and out\-of\-hours support when required. **Clinical Systems Support** * Support Electronic Health Record (EHR) and Patient Administration Systems (PAS). * Assist users with clinical applications, medical devices, and healthcare software. * Ensure minimal disruption to patient\-facing services during system outages or maintenance. * Coordinate with clinical departments during system upgrades and deployments. **Infrastructure \& Network Support** * Support Windows desktop and server environments. * Troubleshoot LAN, WAN, Wi\-Fi, VPN, DNS, DHCP, and network connectivity issues. * Monitor system performance and proactively identify potential issues. * Support backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity processes. **Microsoft 365 \& Cloud Services** * Administer Microsoft 365 services including Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive. * Manage user accounts through Active Directory and Entra ID. * Support cloud\-based applications and services. * Assist with endpoint management using Microsoft Intune. **Cybersecurity \& Compliance** * Adhere to healthcare information security policies and procedures. * Support endpoint security, antivirus, and vulnerability management initiatives. * Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and data protection requirements, including: * GDPR * JCI * Health Information and Quality Authority (HIQA) standards (Ireland) * Hospital cybersecurity policies * Maintain strict confidentiality regarding patient and staff information. **Device Deployment \& Asset Management** * Configure and deploy laptops, desktops, tablets, mobile devices, and clinical workstations. * Maintain accurate IT asset inventories. * Perform software installations, upgrades, and patch management. * Support device lifecycle management and hardware refresh projects. **Documentation \& Continuous Improvement** * Create and maintain technical documentation and knowledge base articles. * Document system changes and support procedures. Contribute to IT projects, migrations, and service improvement initiatives. * **Essential Qualifications** * Degree, diploma, or equivalent experience in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related discipline. * Minimum 3 years' experience in IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support, or Infrastructure Support. Full driving licence may be required for multi\-site hospital support. * **Essential Technical Skills** * Windows 10/11 administration * Microsoft 365 administration * Active Directory and Entra ID * Microsoft Intune and endpoint management * Hardware and software troubleshooting * Networking fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN) * Remote support tools * ITSM/Ticketing systems * Basic cybersecurity knowledge MATER PRIVATE NETWORK IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES EMPLOYER

title: IT Administrator (JD-0033)
description: **Overview** An IT Administrator is responsible for managing and maintaining an organization’s computer systems/ end points, networks, and IT infrastructure to ensure optimal performance, security, and user support. This role involves installing, upgrading, troubleshooting hardware and software, implementing security protocols, providing technical support, and coordinating IT operations across the organization. **Key Responsibilities** * Monitor and maintain computer systems to ensure optimal performance and uptime. * Install, upgrade, configure, and maintain hardware, software, and related IT equipment. * Implement security protocols including antivirus, data backup, and disaster recovery plans. * Manage user accounts, permissions, and access control to safeguard company data. * Provide technical support to end\-users for hardware, software, and network issues. * Perform diagnostic testing, troubleshooting, and debugging of IT systems. * Document IT processes, configurations, and troubleshooting procedures. * Implement IT policies and procedures to improve system efficiency and security. * Assist in onboarding new employees with necessary IT setups and training. * Stay updated with current technology trends and recommend upgrades or improvements. * Collaborate with vendors and external service providers as needed. **Required Skills And Qualifications** * B. Tech or equivalent * Relevant certifications such as Microsoft Certified IT Professional, CompTIA Network\+, or Cisco certifications are preferred * 8 \+ years of experience in IT administration or a similar role. * Strong knowledge of network infrastructure, operating systems (Windows, Linux, MacOS), and enterprise software. * Experience with server management, virtualization, and cloud services is a plus. * Excellent troubleshooting, problem\-solving, and organizational skills. * Good communication skills and ability to work independently or within teams. **Remuneration:** As per industry standards **Why Join Us:** At Sant Nirankari Health City, we offer a collaborative and supportive work environment where your contributions are valued and recognized. Join us in our mission to deliver exceptional healthcare services while upholding the highest standards of quality and patient care. Apply Now ← Back to Careers Page

title: IT Airport Engineer - Dublin Airport
description: **IT Airport Engineer \- Dublin Airport** Location: Dublin Airport, Ireland Contract Duration: 6\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract Salary: €30,000 per annum plus shift allowance €500 Hours: Rotational shift pattern covering 24x7 airport operation **About ESP Global Services** ESP Global Services is a leading global provider of technology support and managed services, delivering critical IT and infrastructure solutions across multiple industries worldwide. We are seeking Airport Engineers to join our dedicated on\-site team at Dublin Airport, supporting passenger processing technologies that are essential to airport and airline operations. This is an exciting opportunity to work in a fast\-paced, operationally critical environment where technology availability directly impacts the passenger journey. **About the Role** As an Airport Engineer, you will provide first\-line technical support, maintenance and operational assistance for passenger processing systems across the airport estate. You will be responsible for ensuring that passenger check\-in, self\-service kiosk and automated bag\-drop technologies remain fully operational and available to support airport operations. Working within a dedicated engineering team, you will diagnose and resolve hardware issues, perform preventative maintenance, support installations and upgrades, manage spare parts inventories and maintain accurate asset records. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide first\-line technical support for passenger processing systems, including: + Common Use Passenger Processing Systems (CUPPS) + Common Use Self\-Service (CUSS) Kiosks + Self\-Service Bag Drop (SSBD) equipment * Diagnose and resolve hardware faults, equipment failures and peripheral issues. * Perform preventative maintenance, inspections and cleaning activities to maximise equipment availability. * Replace and repair hardware components and field\-replaceable units. * Respond to incidents, service requests and operational alerts through the designated IT Service Management (ITSM) platform. * Support equipment installations, moves, additions and changes (IMAC activities). * Maintain accurate records of all supported equipment, including asset tags, serial numbers, locations, hardware changes, warranty information and maintenance history within the Configuration Management Database (CMDB), asset management and service management systems. * Ensure all equipment moves, additions, changes, repairs and replacements are accurately recorded and traceable throughout the asset lifecycle. * Manage spare parts inventory, warranty returns and stock control processes. * Work closely with airport operations, service desk and technical support teams to maintain agreed service levels. * Support continuous improvement initiatives and operational readiness across the airport environment. **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Previous experience in a desktop support, field engineering, hardware support or technical maintenance role. * Strong hardware troubleshooting and break\-fix experience. * Experience supporting Windows\-based devices, printers, scanners, touchscreens or self\-service technologies. * Experience using ServiceNow and/or other IT Service Management (ITSM) platforms. * Experience working within ticket\-driven support environments and to service level agreements (SLAs). * Ability to work independently in a customer\-facing operational environment. * Strong communication, organisational and problem\-solving skills. * Full driving licence and ability to travel between airport locations where required. **Desirable Only** * Experience within an airport, aviation, transport or other operationally critical environment. * Experience supporting kiosks, passenger processing systems or self\-service technologies. * Experience maintaining IT asset inventories, equipment records and Configuration Management Databases (CMDBs). * Experience supporting asset lifecycle management and inventory control processes. * Previous airside operational experience. **Shift Pattern** This role forms part of a dedicated engineering team providing support across a 24x7 airport operation. Candidates must be flexible to work a rotating shift pattern, including days, nights, weekends and public holidays, as required to maintain service coverage and operational resilience. **Security \& Airside Requirements** Due to airport security and airside access requirements, all successful candidates must be capable of obtaining and maintaining an airport security pass. Applicants must be able to provide a fully verifiable five\-year employment, education and address history. Any significant unexplained gaps, or periods that cannot be satisfactorily evidenced through references or supporting documentation, may affect eligibility for airport security clearance and airside access approval. **What We Offer** * Annual Salary of €30,000 plus shift allowance (pro\-rated) * Comprehensive product and technical training. * Opportunity to work within one of Ireland's busiest and most dynamic operational environments. * Experience supporting business\-critical passenger processing technologies. * Opportunity to work alongside a dedicated team of airport technology professionals. * Exposure to airport operations, infrastructure and passenger\-facing technologies. * Potential contract extension opportunities based on business requirements and performance. If you are a customer\-focused engineer who enjoys solving technical problems in fast\-paced operational environments and wants to play a key role in keeping passengers moving, we would like to hear from you. ESP Global Services is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified candidates.

title: IT Business Analyst
description: **Company Description** ***Why are we here?*** Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate. Eurofins is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across the entire network of Eurofins companies. **Job Description** **In a nutshell** The IT Business Analyst acts as the key bridge between business stakeholders and IT teams, ensuring business needs are translated into effective technical solutions. You will gather, analyse, and document requirements, working closely with Product Managers and development teams to deliver high\-quality systems. The role plays a critical part in driving alignment, collaboration, and successful IT project delivery across regions. **Where will you be based and what business will you support?** Based in Dublin, Ireland. You will be part of a global IT organisation, supporting business units, laboratory operations, and key stakeholders across regions, ensuring IT solutions align with business and operational goals. **How can you help us?** By acting as the key liaison between business and IT, ensuring requirements are clear, structured, and actionable. You will support successful project delivery through strong collaboration, effective requirement management, and hands\-on involvement throughout implementation and post go\-live phases. **What can we do for you?** We offer the opportunity to work on impactful, global IT projects within a collaborative and international environment, providing exposure to business\-critical systems, continuous learning, and professional growth opportunities. **Qualifications** **Are you our kind of extraordinary?** **You have:** * Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, or related field * Proven experience in business and technical requirements analysis * Experience with Azure DevOps * Experience working on ERP implementations * Background in healthcare or clinical environments is a plus * English proficiency: minimum B2\+, ideally C1 **Additional Information** **We support your development!** Do you feel like you don’t match 100% of the requirements? Don’t hesitate to apply anyway! Eurofins companies are committed to supporting your career development. **We embrace diversity!** As an Equal Opportunity Employer, the Eurofins network of companies believes in strength and innovation through diversity. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique. Find out more on our Careers page: https://careers.eurofins.com/ **Company description:** Eurofins Scientific is an international network of independent life sciences companies, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic and labelling is accurate. The Eurofins network of companies believes that it is a global leader in food, environment, pharmaceutical and cosmetic product testing and in discovery pharmacology, forensics, advanced material sciences and agroscience contract research services. It is also one of the market leaders in certain testing and laboratory services for genomics, and in the support of clinical studies, as well as in biopharma contract development and manufacturing. It also has a rapidly developing presence in highly specialised and molecular clinical diagnostic testing and in\-vitro diagnostic products. In under 40 years, Eurofins has grown from one laboratory in Nantes, France to over 65,000 employees across a decentralised and entrepreneurial network of over 950 laboratories in over 1,000 independent companies across 59 countries. Eurofins companies offer a portfolio of over 200,000 analytical methods to evaluate the safety, identity, composition, authenticity, origin, traceability and purity of biological substances and products. The Eurofins network generated EUR 7\.296 billion revenues in FY 2025, and has been one of the fastest growing listed European companies for almost three decades.

title: In-Game Specialist (DNA)
description: **Who We Are** At 2K, we create some of the most iconic and culture\-shaping video games in entertainment, including ***NBA® 2K***, one of the top\-selling franchises in the world, and legendary titles like ***BioShock®****,* ***Borderlands®****,* ***Mafia****,* ***Sid Meier's Civilization*****®**, and***XCOM®***, as well as fan favorites ***WWE® 2K****,* ***TopSpin®***, and ***PGA TOUR® 2K****.* We build unforgettable experiences by pushing the boundaries of creativity, authenticity and innovation across every genre. Our portfolio is brought to life by some of the most influential game development studios in the world. **Visual Concepts, Firaxis Games, Hangar 13, Cat Daddy Games, 31st Union, Cloud Chamber, Gearbox, HB Studios,** and **2K SportsLab** create world\-class experiences across platforms. But what truly powers 2K is our people. We believe the best ideas come from teams that feel empowered, supported, and inspired. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive workplace where people are encouraged to **come as they are** and do their best work. **What We Need** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In this remit, the specialist also plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing to ensure successful delivery of in\-game messaging and storefronts. A critical component of this P4 role involves scaling in\-game DNA tech capabilities in partnership with 2KT to unlock greater commercial levers as well as driving the continuous refinement of team processes to ensure successful delivery at scale. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What You'll Do** The In\-Game Specialist is the operational and strategic lead for the in\-game messaging lifecycle within DNA titles. The Specialist operationalizes the in\-game weekly ad schedule and storefront offers. This role is responsible for the end\-to\-end orchestration of targeted marketing and lifecycle campaigns across messaging and commerce, including but not limited to upsell and player engagement journeys, partnership activations, and locker code delivery. Acting as a central hub between Marketing, Product, Insights, Joint Tech (T2/2KT) and Game Studios. In addition, the specialist plays an important role in the coordination of pre\- and post\-launch in\-game QA testing and the scaling of in\-game DNA tech capabilities. This individual provides functional mentorship to rotating graduates while driving operational excellence across the Owned Media team. **What Will Make You A Great Fit** Ad/Storefront Operations \& Strategy * Strategy \& Scheduling: Execute the weekly in\-game messaging calendar and set up in\-game storefront offers, as well as supporting a wide range of targeted upsell and engagement activations. * End\-to\-End Operationalization: Manage the technical setup, scheduling, and deployment of all in\-game messaging and storefronts, ensuring 100% accuracy in a high\-stakes, live\-service environment. * Performance Optimization: Partner with the Insights team to understand impact of in\-game interventions and refine in\-game strategy. Process Refinement \& Scalability * Workflow Optimization: Evaluate and refine existing team processes to eliminate bottlenecks and ensure the successful delivery of activations as the portfolio and complexity scale. * Standardization: Develop and maintain standardized templates, documentation, and playbooks for in\-game ad and storefront operations. * Scalability Initiatives: Identify and implement tools or automated solutions that increase the team's capacity to manage high\-volume activations. DNA Tech Scalability * Strategy \& Workflow Optimization: Identifying strategic opportunities to unlock commercial \& engagement levers and streamline internal workstreams through DNA tech. * Tech Briefing: Submitting detailed tech briefs for new feature development \& improvements to existing features and submitting bugs on an ongoing basis to develop DNA tech capabilities. * Scalability \& Standardization: Ensure long term scalability of all tech requests and standardization across all DNA integrated titles. Product/Marketing \& Developer Collaboration * Product/Marketing Integration: Collaborate closely with the BU Product \& Marketing teams to synchronize in\-game activations with external campaigns, partnerships, and cross\-sell initiatives. * Studio Developer Liaison: Act as the primary operational point of contact for studios such as HB Studios, Hangar 13, VC (WWE), and Firaxis. * Technical Troubleshooting: Work directly with developer studios on bug identification and the continuous optimization of in\-game tech to improve the player experience. Functional Leadership * Mentorship: Oversee the day\-to\-day work of rotating graduates, providing task\-level guidance and ensuring operational excellence. * Functional Expert: Serve as the subject matter expert for in\-game operations, advising internal stakeholders on feasibility and best practices. Core Competencies * Behavioral: Strong sense of urgency and ownership; ability to maintain high precision under tight weekly deadlines. * Functional / Technical: Expert knowledge of in\-game messaging \& commerce technology (Technodrome), targeted campaign frameworks, and tech briefing. * Operational Excellence \& Scalability: Proven ability to build scalable workflows and improve organizational efficiency. * Strategic Influence: Ability to align cross\-functional stakeholders (Marketing, Product, Dev, Insights) toward a unified in\-game strategy. * Collaboration: Proven ability to build strong working relationships with marketing, product, and technical teams alike. Required Qualifications, Knowledge, and Job\-Related Skills * Extensive experience in digital operations, live\-service gaming, or in\-game messaging management. * Proven experience in process improvement or operational scaling within a marketing or technical environment. * Track record of managing a high\-volume, weekly operational cadence. * Experience in working with technical teams to deliver scalable UI \& functionality product improvements * Understanding of video game launch \& release management processes and experience working with Functional QA teams to validate player\-facing product features * Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data insights and pivot strategy accordingly * Excellent project management skills, specifically in coordinating between Marketing and Technical/Development teams. * Self\-motivated with the ability to operate independently at a P4 seniority level. Preferred Qualifications * Passion for our player's experience in\-game and an understanding of video games, as well as key marketing principles. * Experience working within a global publishing organization. As an equal opportunity employer, we are committed to ensuring that qualified individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform their essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us if you need reasonable accommodation. Please note that 2K Games and its studios never uses instant messaging apps or personal email accounts to contact prospective employees or conduct interviews and when emailing, only use 2K.com accounts. \#LI\-Hybrid

title: Incident Management Engineer (Spanish/English bilingual), Incident Detection and Response
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon has built a reputation for excellence with a mission to be the earth’s most customer\-centric company, a company that customers from all over the globe will recognize, value, and trust for both our products and our service. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is carrying on that tradition while leading the world in cloud technologies. The AWS Incident Detection and Response team is part of the ASPIRE organisation within AWS Support, and is dedicated to offering eligible AWS Enterprise Support customers proactive engagement and incident management to reduce the potential for failure and to accelerate recovery of critical workloads from disruption. We achieve these objectives by working closely with customers to develop runbooks and response plans customized to the context of each workload onboarded to the service. Onboarded workloads are monitored 24x7 by a team of Incident Management Engineers (IMEs) to detect and engage customers on a call bridge within 5 minutes of a critical alarm. ABOUT YOU Incident Management Engineers have a broad skill set with demonstrated career progression and a proven track record of delivering results. The successful candidate will possess strong analytical acumen, solid technology experience, superb business judgment, strategic account ownership and a propensity to dive deep to solve complex problems. You will also have a passion for creating/providing a world class experience for our customers. The candidate must understand the competitive and industry landscape and must have the leadership presence and communication skills to effectively work with customers at all levels of their organization. You must be a self\-starter and able to execute at both a tactical and strategic level – with a strong attention to detail. This is a global role that requires excellent written and verbal communication skills and a passion and desire for leading the resolution of critical incidents. Your decisions are not only fundamental to helping protect our most critical customers but will help maintain the health of AWS customers worldwide. Finally, you are passionate about technology with a desire to learn more and do more with AWS. ABOUT THE ROLE AWS Support is looking for a leader with a strong background in Incident Management and customer ownership to be there during the moments that matter for our most critical customers. We are looking for an Incident Management Engineer to join our team to provide incident response and account ownership. In this position, you will play a pivotal role in providing communication, emergency response, technical resolver engagement and incident management for our customers. Please note as a follow\-the\-sun organisation, IMEs work the core hours of 7am\-3pm UTC. Successful applicants will be required to work some weekends (Sunday to Thursday, or Tuesday to Saturday), and public holidays. Key job responsibilities * Every day will bring new and exciting challenges that include elements of: * Drive the resolution of large scale customer impacting incidents as part of a team rotation * Drive critical, complex customer escalations in situations that are sometimes technically challenging in collaboration with Engineering Teams. * Provide critical incident response/management (including leading calls with internal/external participants) for customer’s critical workloads * Contribute to Problem Records for customers * Conduct continuous real\-time proactive monitoring of customer metrics * Prioritize, manage, and own emerging and developing customer issues from start to finish * Monitor and manage communications during high impact events via relevant channels * Collaborate with key stakeholders across AWS to improve the customer experience and develop mechanisms that support operational excellence * Lead projects and teams to drive operational improvements * Create and review documentation; design/influence new standard operating procedures * Identify and troubleshoot recurring platform issues and own projects to drive improvements * Mentor peers in your areas of technical and operational strength * Perform other duties as required by the organization About the team Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. **BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Speak, write, and read fluently in Spanish * Bachelor's degree in computer science or equivalent, or experience in technical support * 4\+ years of technical support experience * 3\+ years demonstrable Major Incident / Problem Manager Experience for organizations that run mission critical applications * 4\+ years of experience in Support Engineering, Network Engineering, Solutions Architecture, or similar IT role **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Industry specific accredited certification(s) such as the AWS Associate level certifications * Knowledge of ITIL/Lean Processes * Ability to work in ambiguous environments * Demonstrated critical thinking and logical problem solving skills * Experience driving collaborative projects from conception to delivery * Familiarity operating or designing distributed architectures with the ability to correlate system behaviors based on known inter\-dependencies * Ability to review complex technical details regarding ongoing issues/events and convey the key details to senior stakeholders to facilitate real\-time decision making Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Information Technology Specialist
description: **SENIOR ICT SPECIALIST (HIGHER EXECUTIVE OFFICER)** The State Laboratory is seeking a permanent Senior ICT Specialist to support and enhance its ICT infrastructure, applications and cybersecurity environment. Reporting to the ICT Section Manager, the successful candidate will play a key role in delivering ICT projects, driving service improvements and ensuring the reliability and security of business\-critical systems. **Key responsibilities include:** * Support and maintain ICT services comprising network, storage, compute, virtualisation, endpoint and cloud services. * Perform infrastructure incident management, escalation and response. * Champion security best practices, taking a lead role in security applications including endpoint protection, mail filters, web filters and firewalls. * Assist the ICT Manager in the development and execution of the Cybersecurity Strategy * Coordinate, support and review assigned work of ICT support staff where required. **Essential Requirements:** Given the technical nature of the role to be filled, you must have a relevant qualification which is matched with experience appropriate to this Senior ICT Specialist role. It is up to you to make sure that your CV showcases the experience you have gained so far. Accordingly, you must, on or before Thursday, 16th July 2026 meet one of the following sets of criteria: * Hold a degree qualification at Level 7 on the National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) * major award (i.e. an ordinary level degree), or higher, in a relevant computing or * computational discipline and at least 3 years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and * cyber security experience from your employment to date. * OR * Hold a degree qualification at Level 8 on the NFQ major award (i.e. honours degree), or * higher, with computing or computational modules taken in the final year and at least 3 * years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber security experience from your * employment to date. * OR * Hold a major award qualification of at least Level 6, or higher, on the National Framework of * Qualifications (NFQ) in a relevant computing or computational discipline, for example: an * NFQ Level 6 qualification from a Post Leaving Cert Course at an ETB, or the FIT Tech * Apprenticeship and at least 5 years directly relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber * security experience from your employment to date. * OR * Have achieved at least two industry\-recognised certifications one of which must be in an * area relevant to this role (see page 5 for a range of examples) and at least 5 years directly * relevant\* hands\-on ICT networks and cyber security experience from your employment to * date. * * \*Examples of relevant experience should be appropriate to the role and include, but are not limited to, areas such as networking or cyber security solution implementation, configuration and support/ administration including WAN/ LAN, enterprise firewalls and perimeter defence infrastructure, and penetration testing. **How to Apply** 1\. Download the Information Booklet below: **Job Information Booklet (Download)** 2\. Candidates must apply with their CV via the link below. **Apply Here** \*We require a CV and the completed online application form to be submitted in order for your application to be accepted **Closing Date** 16/07/2026 – 17:00 Applications will not be accepted after the closing date

title: Institutional Research Data Analyst
description: **Department:** Institutional Research Office **Vacancy ID:** 042483 **Closing Date:** 14\-Jul\-2026 Maynooth University is committed to a strategy in which the primary University goals of excellent research and scholarship and outstanding education are interlinked and equally valued. We are seeking an Institutional Research Data Analyst to work within the Office for Institutional Research \& Data Insights. This Office, led by the Director of Office for Institutional Research \& Data Insights, has a responsibility for the development, management and analysis of data to support university planning and decision\-making. The person appointed will work to support the increasing data reporting needs and requirements of the University. This includes supporting the delivery of the University Strategic Plan, contributing to external reporting obligations, enhancing the use of data in quality reviews, and responding to increasing data needs related to equality initiatives. The Institutional Research Data Analyst plays a key role in supporting the university's strategic planning, informed decision\-making, and institutional effectiveness. This position involves the collection, analysis, interpretation, and dissemination of institutional data to internal and external stakeholders. Using a variety of tools and methodologies, this candidate will deliver accurate, timely, and actionable insights to support academic, administrative, and strategic functions across the University **Salary** **Administrative Officer II (2026\):** €47,387 – €67,129 p.a. (9 points) Appointments will be made in accordance with public sector pay provisions. **Closing Date** 23:30hrs (local Irish time) on **Tuesday, 14th July 2026\.** Applications must be submitted by the closing date and time specified above. Any applications which are still in progress at the closing time on the specified closing date will be cancelled automatically by the system. Late applications will not be accepted. **Maynooth University is an equal opportunities employer** **The position is subject to the Statutes of the University**

title: Insurance Apprenticeship - Portlaoise
description: Based in our Wexford office, Howden Ireland are delighted to partner with the Atlantic Technological University Sligo and the Insurance Institute of Ireland for the Insurance apprenticeship programme. If you would like to join the apprenticeship programme, we would love to hear from you. Find out more below: About the Apprenticeship Opportunity We are looking for ambitious and talented individuals who show the potential to develop into a high performing business professional. This is an apprentice scheme that will allow the successful candidate exposure to all aspects of our business, allowing you to develop your technical expertise as well as professional skills. While on the programme, in addition to gaining on the job insurance experience, you will complete the insurance industry professional qualifications APA and CIP as well as a Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Insurance Practice (Apprenticeship) which is Level 8 on the NFQ awarded by IT Sligo. The apprenticeship programme is a work\-based learning programme and requires timely submission of on\-the\-job assessments, attendance at and successful attainment of IT Sligo and CIP exams as part of the programme. When you join Howden, you will be assigned a dedicated business supervisor and mentor to provide support throughout the scheme while always been given on the job continuous training. We will work with you to become a valuable contributor in the success of our business, developing your own rewarding career in the insurance industry while completing the three\-year Level 8 Bachelor's Degree course. In return, we expect you to bring the commitment and drive to add significant value to the company. About the Role: As an apprentice you will gain in\-depth knowledge of risks and the insurance market in order to arrange suitable policies \& cover for your clients. You will learn how to be an effective intermediary between our clients and insurance companies. Some Responsibilities are noted below: * Gather information from your clients, assessing their insurance needs and risk profile * Research insurance companies' policies and negotiate with underwriters to find the most suitable insurance for your clients at the best price * Ensure that your clients understand the terms and the extent of the cover provided in line with industry regulations * Foresee your clients' insurance needs, such as policy renewals or amendments * Advise your clients on whether and when they need to make a claim on their policies * Arrange specialised types of insurance cover in complex cases; this may involve preparing reports for insurance underwriters and surveyors and negotiating with insurers * Advise your clients on risk management and help to devise new ways to mitigate risks, for example, by adding security measures such as fencing, surveillance cameras or lighting to commercial properties to reduce the likelihood of a break\-in * Build and maintain ongoing relationships with your clients The Apprenticeship programme is a work\-based learning programme and requires timely submission of on the job assessments, attendance at and successful attainment of ATU Sligo and CIP exams as part of the programme. About You When you work in insurance your customers place a lot of trust and responsibility in your hands, so its essential that you have the necessary knowledge and skills to give them peace of mind. Some of the key attributes that the individual should have are: * Ability to communicate effectively (in both written and verbal form) * Eager to learn and want to develop within an exciting industry * Proactive approach to building and managing customer relationships * Ability to prioritise and work under time pressures * Focused, Diligent and committed to the apprenticeship and company * Excellent PC skills \- Microsoft Packages * Willing to be part of a team while working independently If you want to be part of a dynamic work environment while being rewarded for your creativity, innovation and determination, start your career with Howden. Key Questions asked before applying Interested individuals should ask themselves the following questions before applying: * Do I meet the minimum entry level requirements? Entry\-Requirements\-Updated.pdf (iii.ie) * Can I commit to a three\-year contract of apprenticeship? * Do I have the self\-motivation and time management skills to study at a BA (Hons) level and work at the same time? * Can I take ownership and accountability for my own actions and responsibilities? Application Process * Please submit your application via our careers page * Planned start date: beginning of August 2026 or sooner if available * Closing date for applications is the 7th of July About Howden Ireland Howden Ireland is part of Howden, the global insurance intermediary group, and has over 600 employees across 29 local offices advising clients on their commercial insurance, personal insurance, health insurance, life and pensions and mortgage requirements. Our success is primarily down to the incredible people we employ. For more information, please visit About Us \| Howden Ireland (howdengroup.com)

title: Join the Jentic Talent Community
description: **Description** **About Jentic** At Jentic, we’re on a mission to build the core integration layer for AI, enabling developers to connect their AI systems to the world’s APIs easily and securely. We’re creating a critical missing piece of the future AI tech stack — middleware designed from the ground up for the AI era. We’re not just building an AI\-facing product, but an AI\-native company. We’re building a small highly\-productive team, in which everyone is amplified with AI, and where everyone shares personal and financial ownership in our mission. Joining Jentic means shaping the future of software in a team that is built for the future. **What We’re Building** Jentic is the standalone integration layer that connects AI agents with other systems and each other. Built by developers, for developers, our platform simplifies and accelerates API integration, increasing AI agent reliability, while solving security and access control challenges. **Requirements** **About Our Jentic Talent Community** Thank you for your interest in Jentic. We may not have a position right now that currently matches to your experience and expertise, however we would love if you shared your CV with us so we can get in touch with you about future roles when the time is right. Please note that being part of our Jentic Talent Community does not mean that you are actively applying for a role with us. It means that you'll be in our talent list so that when the right role comes up, we are able to contact you. **Benefits** **Why Join Us Now?** * Be a pioneer: Join an exceptionally experienced founding team at a critical stage in Jentic’s journey. Early team members will play a foundational role in the company’s growth and direction. * Mark your mark: Help define the core architecture of the future AI stack and make your mark during this unprecedented platform shift to AI. * Developer\-focused mission: Build tools that solve real\-world problems developers face when integrating AI agents. * AI\-native culture: Learn how to leverage AI to multiply your effectiveness and help create an organisation that reflects what businesses will look like in the years to come. **Take a First Class Ticket on the AI Train** This is your chance to work on a critical missing piece of the future of AI in a startup led by seasoned founders with a track record of success. Join us in building the essential middleware for AI, and help shape the future of software during the most transformative shift in decades. Ready to get on board? Apply now and let’s build the future together. **Equal Opportunity Employer Statement** Jentic is an equal opportunity employer, and we value diversity in the workplace. We are committed to providing an inclusive environment for all employees and candidates. We don't just tolerate differences—we celebrate them. Our strength comes from perspectives that challenge the status quo.

We value open communication, collaboration, and mutual respect. title: Junior Architectural Technologist - Dublin Studio
description: **Role:** Graduate Architectural Technologist **Client:** Highly Regarded Irish Studio **Location:** Dublin City **Salary:** Competitive Package **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded architectural practice with offices across Ireland. They have a strong track record delivering projects across the Commercial, Residential, Education and Hospitality sectors, and have been in business for over two decades. With a growing project pipeline and an expanding Dublin studio, they offer an excellent opportunity for an ambitious Architectural Technologist to develop their career within a collaborative and supportive environment. **The Role** Our client is seeking a Junior or Graduate Architectural Technologist to join their growing Dublin team, supporting the delivery of projects across a range of sectors. Working alongside experienced Designers, you will assist in the production of technical drawings and BIM models, contributing to projects from planning through to construction. This role offers excellent exposure to all stages of project delivery while providing ongoing mentoring and support to help develop your technical and professional skills. The successful candidate will have the opportunity to work within a collaborative studio environment, gaining hands\-on experience on a variety of high\-quality projects while developing their Revit and technical detailing capabilities. **Required Background** * BSc (Hons) in Architectural Technology or a related discipline * Graduate or up to 2 years' professional experience within an architectural practice * Proficiency in Revit and a good understanding of BIM principles * Strong technical aptitude and an eagerness to develop construction detailing skills * Exposure to the Irish or UK construction industry advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Collaborative approach with the ability to work as part of a multidisciplinary team * Well organised, with the ability to manage deadlines and prioritise workload * Enthusiastic, proactive and keen to build a long\-term career in Architectural Technology **The Package** Excellent salary on offer, as well as 23 days annual leave, up to 2 days WFH post\-probation and other perks.
