No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** At Distilled, we're looking for a **Junior IT Operations / L1 Helpdesk Engineer** to join our team and help deliver a fantastic technology experience for our employees. This is a hands\-on role focused on supporting our people, maintaining our office technology, and ensuring employees have the tools and support they need to do their best work. You'll work closely with the IT Operations team, providing day\-to\-day support across hardware, software, meeting rooms, onboarding, and office technology while developing your skills across modern cloud\-based platforms such as Google Workspace, Okta, Jamf, Microsoft 365 and Freshservice. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving problems, helping people, learning new technologies and taking ownership of their work. **Please note:** This role requires **4 days per week onsite** at our centrally located Dublin office, with flexibility to work remotely on the remaining day. **What will I be doing** As a Junior IT Operations Engineer, you'll play a key role in supporting employees and maintaining the smooth operation of our technology environment. You'll work across a wide variety of technologies while building your skills and experience in a supportive team environment. * **Employee Support \& Service Delivery:** Providing remote and onsite IT support for employees across the business, troubleshooting hardware, software, application and device issues and responding to both walk\-up requests and helpdesk tickets. Assisting employees with Google Workspace, Okta, Microsoft 365 and other business applications, maintaining high levels of customer service and communication throughout, and escalating issues appropriately when required. * **Onboarding, Offboarding \& User Lifecycle:** Preparing laptops and equipment for new starters, including purchasing and shipping as needed. Assisting with employee onboarding and offboarding activities, including creating and updating user accounts, groups and permissions. Ensuring devices, applications and access are ready for Day 1, while maintaining accurate records of employee equipment and access. * **Office Technology \& Meeting Rooms:** Maintaining meeting room technology, AV equipment and shared workspaces, supporting company meetings, presentations and events. Troubleshooting issues with displays, cameras, microphones and video conferencing systems, ensure hotdesks and shared technology areas remain operational and well maintained, and be a visible and approachable IT presence in the office. * **Asset Management \& Inventory:** Maintaining accurate IT asset inventory records and assisting with equipment ordering and stock management, tracking hardware throughout its lifecycle. Organise and maintain the IT stock room, ensuring spare equipment and accessories are available when needed. * **Continuous Learning \& Improvement:** Continuously developing skills across the technology stack and contributing to documentation and knowledge base articles. Identify recurring issues and suggest improvements, and support automation and process improvement initiatives where appropriate. **What skills and experience do I need** * Minimum 1 years’ experience in an IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support or IT Operations role * Strong troubleshooting skills across Windows, MacOS or Mobile devices * Experience supporting Microsoft 365, Google Workspace or similar collaboration platforms * Experience with user account management and onboarding/offboarding activities * Experience with supporting video conference room setup * Strong communication and customer service skills * Ability to prioritise workload and manage multiple tasks * Positive attitude and willingness to learn * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail * Comfortable working onsite and interacting directly with employees **Advantage if you have** * Exposure to Intune, Jamf, Okta, Entra ID or other identity/device management platforms * Experience with Google Meet or Zoom Rooms * Experience maintaining asset inventory or equipment lifecycle processes * Basic networking knowledge (Wi\-Fi, VPNs, DNS, DHCP) * Experience with Freshservice, or similar ticketing systems * ITIL Foundation, Jamf 100, Google Workspace, Microsoft or CompTIA certifications **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together**. **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen**. We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: Junior Project Management Coordinator
description: We are adding a Junior Project Management Coordinator to our team to help us manage a series of technical projects for a partner in the international financial services sector. This role is about the practical side of delivery: making sure requirements are clear, keeping project tickets updated, and ensuring that our developers have exactly what they need to get their work done. This is a fully remote position, meaning you will work from your own space and coordinate with us through digital tools. **About Pixel Systems** Pixel Systems is a small software agency that builds the tools teams actually use. We do not build flashy social media apps or marketing landing pages. We focus on the practical stuff like admin panels, CRM integrations, and the database migrations that most teams try to avoid. Our clients come to us when they need to replace a sprawling spreadsheet with a real database or get different software tools to communicate with each other. We believe that working code is better than a perfect plan that never ships. Our approach is straightforward: we provide fixed quotes before we start, we do weekly demos, and we hand over everything when we are finished. This includes the code, the documentation, and even walkthrough videos for the client. We use stable, well\-tested technologies like Node, React, and Postgres because we want the software we build to be reliable. We are a small, distributed team that prefers solving real operational headaches over chasing the latest industry trends. **The Role** As a Junior Project Management Coordinator, your focus will be on the daily flow of information. You will be the person who translates client requests into clear tasks for our development team. Because our partner in the international financial services sector has precise data requirements, you will spend a lot of time organizing specifications and checking that nothing has been missed. Your work keeps our projects from getting bogged down in confusion or technical debt. On a typical day, you might be cleaning up a project board, assisting a lead developer with documentation, or helping to organize a weekly demo for a client. You will not be expected to write code, but you will be expected to understand how our systems work so you can talk about them accurately. This is a permanent, full\-time role where you will be an essential link between the client requirements and the final product. **What you bring** * A high level of organization and an obsession with lists and clear notes. * The ability to write instructions that are easy for others to read and follow. * A patient approach to repetitive tasks, such as updating ticket statuses or checking data consistency. * Comfort working in a fully remote environment using chat and video calls as your primary base for communication. * An interest in how software is built, even if you do not have a technical degree. * The ability to stay calm and helpful when a project timeline needs to be adjusted. * A direct and honest communication style: we prefer hearing about a problem early rather than late. * The capacity to manage your own time without someone looking over your shoulder. **Helpful background** * Experience using basic project tools like Trello, Jira, or similar task tracking software. * A basic understanding of spreadsheets and how to organize data within them. * Exposure to technical environments, perhaps through a bootcamp or personal project. * Knowledge of what an API is or how a database works at a conceptual level. * Experience in a customer service or administrative role where detail was important. * Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar document collaboration tools. **Working at Pixel Systems** Working here is different from working at a large tech company. We have no complex management layers and no office politics to navigate. We focus entirely on shipping work that helps real people, like operations teams and financial analysts. You will be working directly with senior developers and designers who value your input on how to make our internal processes smoother. Since we are 100 percent remote, we value clear, written communication above all else. We do not have meetings just for the sake of having them. Instead, we use our time to solve problems and get the work done. The position is paid, and we provide a level of compensation that is very competitive for entry\-level roles in the current market. You will have the opportunity to learn how a lean software agency operates from the inside out. We are a small team that values a quiet, focused work environment. We do not chase the latest tech fads or try to reinvent the wheel. We simply build stable systems that solve problems for our clients. If you enjoy bring order to chaos and helping a technical team stay on track, you will fit in well here.

title: Junior Project Manger
description: **JLL empowers you to shape a brighter way**. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward. **Role Purpose** We are looking for an enthusiastic, curious Junior Project Manager to join our team based in Leixlip. This role offers hands\-on experience across varying types of construction projects, supporting project managers to deliver capital projects. The role will involve project coordination, site management, quality and EHS. Recent graduates are welcome, and any prior work experience in construction / project management is an advantage. **Role Description** * Support the management of projects from inception to completion, procurement activities, construction management and operations support. * Assist Project Managers with Site operations, Subcontractor management, administration, Tender analysis and Health \& Safety compliance. * Support quality assurance and site inspections to ensure projects meet quality standards. * Track actions, maintain minutes and contractor follow up. * Attend site coordination meetings and liaise with clients, contractors, and suppliers. * Assist with daily Progress reports, Scope development, and general contract administration. * Every day is different and in all these activities, we’d encourage you to show your ingenuity. **Preferred experience** * Minimum of 1 – 2 years’ experience in construction\-based project management role. * Understanding of construction terminology, processes, and project stages. * Positive attitude and willingness to learn is essential. * Independent, resourceful, possess keen ability to learn and with excellent work ethic. **Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Project Management, or a related discipline. **Location:** On\-site –Dublin, IRL If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! At JLL, we harness the power of artificial intelligence (AI) to efficiently accelerate meaningful connections between candidates and opportunities. Using AI capabilities, we analyze your application for relevant skills, experiences, and qualifications to generate valuable insights about how your unique profile aligns with the specific requirements of the role you're pursuing. ***JLL Privacy Notice*** Jones Lang LaSalle (JLL), together with its subsidiaries and affiliates, is a leading global provider of real estate and investment management services. We take our responsibility to protect the personal information provided to us seriously. Generally the personal information we collect from you are for the purposes of processing in connection with JLL’s recruitment process. We endeavour to keep your personal information secure with appropriate level of security and keep for as long as we need it for legitimate business or legal reasons. We will then delete it safely and securely. For more information about how JLL processes your personal data, please view our Candidate Privacy Statement. For additional details please see our career site pages for each country. Jones Lang LaSalle (“JLL”) is an Equal Opportunity Employer and is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the employment process – including the online application and/or overall selection process – you may email us at HRSCLeaves@jll.com. This email is only to request an accommodation. Please direct any other general recruiting inquiries to our Contact Us page \> I want to work for JLL.

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor – Civil Engineering Projects** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a Graduate Quantity Surveyor to join their growing commercial team. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an individual with up to one year's experience to develop their career within a well\-established and highly regarded contractor delivering major infrastructure projects across Dublin and surrounding areas. The successful candidate will work closely with senior commercial staff, gaining exposure to all aspects of quantity surveying on civil engineering and infrastructure projects. The role offers a combination of office and site\-based experience, supporting the delivery of projects across Dublin, Meath, and Kildare, with a flagship project based at Dublin Airport. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the preparation of project cost reports, valuations, and commercial documentation * Support the management and administration of subcontractor accounts * Assist in the preparation and assessment of subcontractor payment applications * Monitor project costs and help identify commercial risks and opportunities * Assist with the preparation and agreement of variations and final accounts * Maintain accurate commercial records and project documentation * Liaise with site management teams, subcontractors, suppliers, and clients * Support monthly cost reporting and forecasting processes * Assist with procurement activities and subcontractor packages * Ensure commercial procedures and contractual requirements are followed throughout the project lifecycle **Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related construction discipline * Ideally 1 year's experience in a Quantity Surveying role, although candidates with relevant work placement experience gained during college will also be considered * Previous experience within civil engineering projects is desirable * Candidates with a general construction background will also be considered * Strong numerical, analytical, and commercial awareness skills * Excellent communication and organisational abilities * Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Full driving licence required **What's on Offer** * Competitive salary (€40,000 – €45,000 DOE) * Company vehicle provided * Opportunity to work with a leading civil engineering contractor * Exposure to large\-scale and varied infrastructure projects, including Dublin Airport **To Apply** If you are a Graduate Quantity Surveyor or recent graduate seeking an opportunity within civil engineering and infrastructure projects, please contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion or send your CV. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com GPC will never send your CV to clients without your permission. GPC is a specialist construction and engineering recruitment consultancy based in Dublin with over 27 years’ experience placing professionals across Ireland and internationally.

title: Junior Sales Assistant
description: **Junior Sales Assistant** **Location:** Naas, Co. Kildare **Job Type:** Full\-Time, Permanent **Salary:** Competitive (Based on Experience) **About Us** At N7 Commercial Centre, we are a growing and dynamic business specialising in commercial vehicles, horseboxes, fleet solutions, and workshop services. Due to continued expansion, we are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic **Junior Sales Assistant** to join our team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to start or develop a career in sales within a fast\-paced and supportive environment. **The Role** As a Junior Sales Assistant, you will support the sales team in delivering exceptional customer service while assisting with vehicle sales enquiries, lead generation, and administrative tasks. **Key Responsibilities** * Assisting customers with sales enquiries in person, over the phone, and via email. * Supporting the sales team with quotations and customer follow\-ups. * Maintaining customer records and updating CRM systems. * Assisting with social media and online vehicle advertisements. * Preparing vehicle documentation and sales packs. * Helping to maintain showroom and forecourt presentation. * Building positive relationships with new and existing customers. * Supporting marketing and promotional activities as required. **Requirements** **Essential** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Good computer literacy (Microsoft Office). * Positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Full clean driving licence. * Ability to work as part of a team. **Desirable** * Previous customer service or sales experience. * Interest in commercial vehicles, automotive, agricultural, or equestrian industries. * Experience using CRM systems. **What We Offer** Full training provided Supportive team environment On\-site parking Opportunity to grow within a successful and expanding company **How to Apply** Please submit your CV along with a short cover letter outlining why you would be a great fit for the role. We look forward to hearing from you. Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Junior Supply Chain Consultant - September 2026
description: **Job Description** **About the role** **Kickstart your consulting career!** Get ready to launch your career in consulting, and join us! You’ll have a spot in our supply chain training boot camp at the beginning of September. You’ll sharpen your consulting expertise alongside fellow new starters from around the globe. Our training program covers everything from essential soft skills to advanced supply chain management to becoming a kick\-ass Supply Chain Consultant. Afterwards, you're ready to start on one of our running projects. **What can you do at Bluecrux?** * You'll start immediately in a visible, autonomous role as a project member in one of our running value chain projects in industries such as life sciences, chemicals, food and beverages, and manufacturing. * You'll see yourself working on and contributing to the main project phases. Here's an idea of what to expect: + Analyze and Optimize: You will always analyze, map, and conduct existing Supply Chain and Operations business processes. + Collaborate: Work closely with clients to understand their needs and make a real impact. Think about preparing and conducting project interviews to understand the client's needs better. You will become aware of the customer organization and be part of the change in their organization and ways of working. + Track and report: You'll track KPIs and report them accurately to the customer project team, ensuring clear, actionable insights. + Document and deliver: You create the most important customer and project deliverables, such as top\-notch project notes, guides and training material. * Here’s how you’ll continuously be making an impact: + Tell us, what’s your flavour? All of our projects share a common transformation mission, and once you join a project, you’ll be introduced to different extra layers of specialisation to add value. We will encourage you to explore and accelerate in Technology, Process knowledge, Data or Change Management. **Job Requirements** **About you** * You will graduate before September 2026 and want to start your career at the beginning of September 2026\. * You see yourself thriving in the world of value chain transformation and are interested in how Technology, Business Processes, Data Analytics, and Change management create a flourishing ecosystem of solutions. * You feel connected with and are ready to represent our company values: dig deep, own it, come together, move fast and be kind! * Above all, we believe in your potential: + You can demonstrate profound logical reasoning, and you are resourceful in detailing solutions and considerate about their consequences. + You're a team worker and player who can perform well without much supervision and who is committed to your own objectives. + You're open to feedback and always look for ways to enhance collaboration. + You like to be challenged daily, show proactive ownership and, above all, thrive in a fast\-paced environment. * Some hard skills: + Ideally, you have had an introduction to Supply Chain Processes in a minor or major within a master’s degree program, such as business engineering, civil engineering—operational research, applied economics, mechanical engineering or an MBA program. + You are fluent in English, the official work language in all of our offices. Mastery of any other language is a definite plus. + Willingness and eligibility to travel globally and to work in a hybrid working model. **Why join us?** * A kickstart to your career through our Supply Chain Bootcamp training program, at the beginning of September. You will learn the ins \& outs of Supply Chain Management: Demand Planning, S\&OP and Financial Planning, Supply Planning, Inventory Management, Master Data Management, Supply Chain Modelling and Planning Tools. This will allow you to onboard into our projects with a backpack full of expertise \& skills! * We know you by your first name, and don't let titles or levels withhold us from being truly aware of your talents. * You'll work on international projects and travel to client sites. From the start, you will gain exposure and recognition for every achievement at Bluecrux. * A Great Place To Work with lots of growth opportunities. We encourage initiative, so you’ll learn a lot quickly. Sometimes, you’ll fail fast, but we’ll help you to get up. We’ll enhance your personal and professional skills, and you can count on a great, supportive team of hands\-on, inspiring and enthusiastic supply chain colleagues. * Team events and fun include yearly Bluecrux weekends, last Friday meetings with drinks, team building, and sports activities. * Enjoy a flexible \& hybrid work environment, combining remote work, office presence (in our brand new office!) and onsite client visits. * A competitive salary package, bonus program, net allowances, a smartphone with phone subscription and other benefits (such as a fridge full of drinks \& healthy snacks). **About us** At Bluecrux, we transform supply chains into smart, efficient value chains through a blend of expert consulting and cutting\-edge technology. We collaborate closely with industry leaders like Johnson \& Johnson, GSK, Bridgestone, AkzoNobel, Beiersdorf, Oatly, and many more – creating partnerships that drive meaningful impact. With a global presence in Europe, the US and APAC, we're committed to shaping the future of value chains, one success story at a time.

title: Junior Technical Analyst - Aviation Advisory
description: The Aviation Advisory team founded in 2016 has since established itself as best in class across its service offerings, growing to a team of 60\+ staff in just 8 years. Current client offerings include; aircraft lease management, data migration management, aircraft maintenance reserve reviews, technical aircraft expertise, deal appraisals for investment decision making, and a broad range of accounting and finance offerings to its clients. The team is currently undertaking lease management for a fleet size of approximately 1,400 leases and provides professional services to 13 out of the Top 15 Aircraft Lessors. **The Opportunity** We have an opportunity for an entry level technical analyst, who is interested in working within the GT Aviation Lease Services team. title: Legal Counsel
description: **About Newcode.ai** Newcode.ai is a fast\-growing legal tech and agentic AI company transforming how legal work is done. With teams across Norway, Sweden, Ireland, the US and growing we work at the intersection of law, technology, and intelligence. We move fast, think big, and take pride in doing things the right way. **The Role** We're looking for a Legal Counsel to build and scale our legal and compliance capabilities as we continue to grow internationally. This role will serve as a strategic partner to leadership, supporting commercial growth, managing corporate governance, protecting company assets, and ensuring compliance across multiple jurisdictions. **Key Responsibilities**: Commercial Contracts* Draft, review, negotiate, and manage customer, vendor, partner, and technology agreements. * Partner with Sales and Customer Success to accelerate deal execution while managing legal risk. * Develop and maintain contract templates, playbooks, and approval processes to improve efficiency and consistency. * Support contract lifecycle management and legal operations initiatives. Corporate \& Governance* Manage corporate governance activities across multiple entities and jurisdictions. * Support equity administration, cap table management, and corporate transactions. * Ensure ongoing compliance with local statutory and regulatory requirements. IP \& Compliance* Maintain and protect the company's intellectual property portfolio, including trademarks, copyrights, and proprietary assets. * Support and manage compliance programs, including ISO certifications, data protection, security, and regulatory frameworks. * Partner with internal stakeholders to identify, assess, and remediate compliance gaps. * Develop and deliver compliance policies, training, and awareness programs. * Experience in Vanta, OneTrust or similar software Risk \& Disputes* Identify, assess, and mitigate legal, regulatory, operational, and commercial risks. * Manage disputes, claims, and litigation matters with external counsel when required. * Provide practical legal guidance to business leaders on risk management and strategic decision\-making. * Maintain risk registers and support company\-wide risk management initiatives. **Requirements** * Law degree from an accredited university in Ireland, Sweden, or Norway * 5\+ years of experience as in house legal counsel preferably at an AI / SaaS tech start ups * Experience supporting commercial contracting and international business operations. * Strong understanding of corporate governance, regulatory compliance, and risk management practices. * Familiarity with ISO standards, privacy regulations, and information security frameworks is highly desirable. * Excellent negotiation, communication, and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to balance legal rigor with business pragmatism in a fast\-paced startup environment. **Benefits** Why You’ll Love Working with Us* **Culture of trust and excellence:** Be part of a team that values reliability, respect, and initiative, where everyone’s contribution matters and diversity drives creativity. * **Collaborative environment:** Work with talented, driven peers on bleeding\-edge legal engineering solutions with real\-world impact. * **Growth and ownership:** Shape your role as we grow. Take ownership and drive outcomes from the start of your journey. * **Inclusive culture of excellence and trust:** We reward initiative, respect, integrity, and teamwork, and are committed to building a global, inclusive team. Your drive, curiosity, and ideas matter more than your background Ready to shape what’s next? Apply today and join us in building a smarter, more efficient world powered by AI.

title: M&E Coordinator
description: PE Global Recruitment are working with a leading Tier 1 organisation in Dublin to recruit an M\&E Coordinator to join the team. This is a full time, permanent role. Role Overview: The M\&E Coordinator will be responsible for the coordination, planning, and delivery of all building services elements across the project, ensuring works are completed safely, on time, and to specification. This role requires close collaboration with the design, construction, and commissioning teams to manage mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) trades, subcontractors, and suppliers. The coordinator will act as the primary interface for building services on site, ensuring compliance with project requirements, regulatory standards, and company procedures. Key Responsibilities: * Coordinate the planning, sequencing, and delivery of all mechanical, electrical, and plumbing works across the project, ensuring alignment with the overall programme. * Act as the point of contact for subcontractors, suppliers, and the project team regarding building services activities. * Review and manage method statements, risk assessments, and permits to ensure all building services works are carried out safely and in compliance with statutory requirements. * Monitor quality of installation works to ensure adherence to project drawings, specifications, and industry standards. * Maintain accurate records of progress, materials, labour, and equipment, reporting to project management on a regular basis. * Support programme management through short\-term look\-ahead planning, identifying potential clashes, constraints, or risks to delivery. * Coordinate commissioning, handover, and testing of all building services systems, ensuring documentation and regulatory compliance are maintained. * Liaise with clients and consultants to address queries, provide updates, and ensure expectations are met. * Ensure housekeeping, site safety, and HSEQ requirements are maintained to the highest standard throughout the project. Qualifications \& Experience: * Relevant mechanical, electrical, or building services qualification * Proven experience coordinating MEP or building services works on commercial or industrial projects. * Experience in project planning, scheduling, and subcontractor management. * Knowledge of health and safety legislation and best practice in construction environments (IOSH or equivalent desirable). * Excellent organisational, communication, and interpersonal skills. * Experience working on multi\-disciplinary teams and liaising with clients, consultants, and subcontractors. Desirable Skills: * Experience on turnkey or fit\-out projects. * Familiarity with Dublin/Ireland building codes and standards. * Proficiency in project management software and BIM coordination. * Experience with commissioning and handover processes for mechanical and electrical systems. Interested candidates should submit an updated CV. Please click the link below to apply, or alternatively send an up\-to\-date CV to michaela.murray@peglobal.net \*\*\*Please note our client cannot assist with any visa sponsorship and candidates must have the correct visa to live and work in Ireland\*\*\*

title: Manufacturing Systems Specialist
description: As a Manufacturing Systems specialist you will play a key role in establishing the Manufacturing Systems team within the Manufacturing Operations group. The incumbent will provide expert support to the manufacturing team in the areas of SAP, LIMS, finite scheduling (Orchestrate) and benchtop equipment. **Essential Duties and Responsibilities** * Key member of Manufacturing Operations team in WuXi Biologics Ireland. * Provide expert support to the Manufacturing department in the areas of SAP, LIMS, Trackwise and Finite Scheduling. * Process and System owner for Manufacturing benchtop equipment. Responsibilities in this area will include but are not limited to management of the MFG benchtop support e.g. vendors, maintain an accurate equipment list of benchtop equipment and ensure all required system SOPs are in place. * Ensure all Manufacturing benchtop equipment are maintained in a qualified state by collaborating with relevant departments on all aspects of benchtop equipment qualification lifecycle (URS to decommissioning). * Co\-ordinate vendor visits on site for bench top support, qualification and maintenance as needed. * Provide input to budget for systems. * Collaborate with MT and MSAT groups on technical transfer and process validation as required. * Collaborate with QC \& QA groups to ensure GMP compliance, manage deviation investigation and CAPA implementation, and support client auditing and agency inspections. * Ensure that the MFG training team are supported in regards to benchtop equipment training and troubleshooting as needed. * Ensuring continual improvement of manufacturing documentation (SOP’s MBR’s, OJT’s) for areas of responsibility to ensure they are current, accurate, and clear. * Communicate operational status regularly to senior management, as required, at the appropriate level of detail. * Assist the MFG systems lead to coordinate the work between the MFG systems groups and other functional groups to meet the production objectives and timelines. * Perform all duties in accordance with GMP requirements, SOPs and controlled documents * Responsible for identifying and assessing new models of benchtop and new equipment as required. * Will be flexible to take on additional tasks and responsibilities at the discretion of the Manufacturing Operations Associate Director and Systems lead. * Support future initiatives such as electronic batch records, electronic logbooks, etc. on behalf of Manufacturing Will act as a role model for the Manufacturing function and also the wider organisation in adherence to the WuXi corporate core values and PROUD culture. **Person Specification** **Technical Competencies** Ideally, experienced in establishing a Manufacturing department and working in a team in a ‘greenfield’ context of similar size and scale. **Experience** * More than 2 years in biopharmaceutical manufacturing. * Experience with GMP systems such as SAP and Benchtop equipment qualification and operation etc. * Ideally have a solid understanding of quality system in terms of deviation, investigation, CAPA and change control. * Experience in working with the cross\-functional teams. * Strong team lead with the demonstrated ability to manage and support manufacturing operations in a flexible and dynamic team environment. **Knowledge** * A proficiency in operating a drug substance commercial manufacturing facility and/or a pilot plant. * The individual need to have good understanding in DQ, FAT, SAT, IQ, OQ, \& PQ (commissioning and qualification). * Knowledge of GMP systems such as SAP and Benchtop equipment qualification and operation etc. * Ideally knowledge and experience of scheduling software including Orchestrate. **Qualifications** * Diploma, BSc, MSc or PhD in Chemical Engineering, Bioscience, Biotechnology or Analytical Science **Behavioural Competencies** * A strong team player with the ability to adapt to daily challenges of a manufacturing work environment while maintaining positive and productive attitude and fostering this attitude among direct reports. * Excellent verbal and written communication and presentation skills. * Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to work effectively with staff at all levels of the organisation. * Ability to work on own initiative and be proactive. * Results oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a short period of time. * Excellent organisational and planning skills and strong attention to detail. As we extend our global reach, we need colleagues interested in change, in challenge, and in building a new future together. Reach beyond yourself and discover your true potential! If you can thrive in a dynamic and fast\-paced environment and feel that the moment has come for you to be part of such an exciting journey towards accomplishing our mission \- Any drug can be made, and any disease can be treated \- don’t miss out on this opportunity to join us and reach beyond yourself and discover your true potential. Apply now! Would you like to know more before you apply? Please visit us at https://www.wuxibiologics.com/join\-us/ WuXi Biologics is an equal opportunities employer.

title: Manufacturing Technician
description: **Job Title:** Manufacturing Engineer **Company:** NanobOx **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **About Us:** NanobOx is a deep\-tech spin\-out from University College Dublin, with highly energy efficient water treatment technology using nanobubbles. Specializing in the generation of nanobubbles from source gases such as air, oxygen, and ozone, we are at the forefront of innovation in the field of water aeration and sanitisation. Our technology has wide application across the bio\-industries and we aim to become a world leader in biological application of nanobubbles. We are seeking a Manufacturing Engineer to join our fast growing team. Please include "Manufacturing Engineering Application" in the subject line. **Application Deadline: 28th February 2023** NanobOx is an equal\-opportunity employer. title: Medical Record Officer (JD-0056)
description: **Overview:** The Medical Record Officer is responsible for the efficient management of patient health records and data. This role ensures that patient medical records are accurate, complete, secure, and maintained in compliance with legal, ethical, and healthcare standards. **Key Responsibilities \& Tasks** * General Administration \& ultimate responsibility of all works concerned with MRD. * Ensure patient records from various departments (OPD, IPD, Emergency, etc.) are timely collected \& updated. * Maintain and update physical and electronic health records (EHR/EMR) accurately and promptly. * Ensure medical records are complete, legible, and meet hospital and regulatory standards. * Ensure confidentiality and security of patient information as per HIPAA or local data protection laws. * Retrieve medical records for doctors, nurses, and administrative personnel as needed. * Assign appropriate medical coding (ICD, CPT) for diagnoses and procedures. * Monitor and ensure timely filing, archiving, and disposal of records in line with hospital policy and legal retention schedules. * Prepare reports, statistics, and summaries related to patient data and hospital performance indicators. * Handle patient and third\-party (insurance/legal) requests for medical records following hospital policy and
