title: Sustainability Graduate
description: Sustainability Graduate, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres and Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Roles\& Responsibilities** * Support the Sustainability Team in scoping, designing, and building out Mercury’s Sustainability strategy, “Our Planet, Our Duty.” * Coordinate ESG data collection, compliance activities, and engage with suppliers where needed. * Support Mercury’s ESG Data and Reporting strategy, including program design, setting goals, identifying priorities, and developing and implementing programs and initiatives. * Benchmark Mercury strategic sustainability ambitions against best\-in\-class industry across all stakeholders; clients, competitors, investors, and others. * Support responses to CDP, Ecovadis, and annual Sustainability Reports aligned with CSRD \& GRI. * Coordinate data processes and timelines for responses to bids, tenders, and supplier requests. * Support Sustainability \& Sales teams in developing clear articulate content for both prequals and tenders that aligns with our client pre\-qual requirements. * Proactive identification of issues, risks, opportunities, and trends that may impact the company from an ESG and corporate responsibility perspective. * Ensure all “Our Planet, Our Duty” initiatives are scoped \& aligned against the ESG framework so we progress in the right direction, can track progress in a clear manner. * All other duties associated with this role and/or reasonably required by the Company. The Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to take into account changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& coordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now Apply Now

title: Systems Engineer, Managed Operations
description: **Description** AWS has successfully launched the European Sovereign Cloud (ESC), marking a significant development in Utility Computing (UC). To spearhead this initiative, we are actively seeking experienced systems engineers with a strong background in automation and operations. As part of the AWS Managed Operations team, you will play a pivotal role in building, operating and evolving operations and development teams dedicated to delivering high\-availability AWS services, including EC2, S3, Dynamo, Lambda, and Bedrock, exclusively for EU customers. For more information on ESC please check out our blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/in\-the\-works\-aws\-european\-sovereign\-cloud/ Your responsibilities will encompass overseeing the ongoing operations and expansion of the ESC, working closely with global AWS teams, and influencing the evolution of AWS services and technology. A typical day in this role involves collaborating with technology leaders, contributing to the enhancement of day\-to\-day operations, and ensuring continuous improvements in availability, reliability, latency, performance, and efficiency of the ESC. You will be required to occasionally participate in “on\-call” rotations to resolve incidents occurring out\-of\-hours. The overarching goal is to deliver scalable services and ensure a high\-availability experience for EU customers. If you are an experienced professional ready for a challenging and impactful opportunity, we invite you to join our efforts in operating and scaling a best\-in\-class development engineering and operations team that aligns with AWS' commitment to customer satisfaction and continual innovation. A day in the life Embark on a week filled with meaningful contributions to the operation and improvement of significant software systems. You dedicate a substantial portion of your time to carefully review the operational health of services within your team's responsibility. In the process, you diligently identify anomalies and craft actionable bug reports, aspiring to enhance the overall efficiency and performance of your systems. In addition to these responsibilities, you offer constructive feedback on change management documents and work earnestly to address your team's operational backlog. Through a collaborative effort, you strive to navigate challenges and ensure the seamless functionality of your systems. Additionally, you engage in the development and testing of scripts, hoping to provide practical solutions to enhance your workflows. Beyond the technical aspects, you assume a role as an educator, sharing insights on the complexities of the European Sovereign Cloud with service teams. It's a humbling experience for you to contribute to the collective knowledge of the team, fostering a culture of mutual understanding. This week encapsulates your commitment to continuous learning and improvement, acknowledging that every effort, no matter how small, contributes to the collective success of your team and the reliability of your software systems. In addition to these responsibilities, your position involves 24x7 on\-call responsibility. You work as a team to root\-cause issues and ensure your systems remain resilient and fault\-tolerant, underscoring your commitment to maintaining operational excellence. Eligibility requirement * Fluency in written and spoken English is required. * Candidate must be a national of an EU member state and residing in the EU to operate the AWS European Sovereign Cloud. * Amazon will provide relocation support for successful applicants relocating within the European Union. * Employees will participate in an On\-Call rota. **About The Team** Utility Computing (UC) European Sovereign Cloud (ESC) is a part of AWS Utility Computing (UC). AWS Utility Computing (UC) provides product innovations — from foundational services such as Amazon’s Simple Storage Service (S3\) and Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2\), to consistently released new product innovations that continue to set AWS’s services and features apart in the industry. As a member of the UC organization, you’ll support the development and management of Compute, Database, Storage, Internet of Things (Iot), Platform, and Productivity Apps services in AWS. Within AWS UC, Managed Operations engineers engage with AWS customers who require specialized security solutions for their cloud services. Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. **Basic Qualifications** * Experience in Linux OS and network troubleshooting, or experience in networking administration and troubleshooting * Experience in Python, Perl, or another scripting language * This role requires you to be a national of an EU member state **Preferred Qualifications** * Able to execute standard operating procedures and following operational best practices * Experience in Systems engineering, site reliability engineering, building and operating systems at scale * Knowledge or experience operating 24x7 high\-availability, distributed software applications, performance tuning software applications and optimizing fleet utilization and monitoring frameworks (such as CloudWatch, Datadog, Grafana, Elastic or similar) * Knowledge or experience with Infrastructure as Code, (such as CDK, CloudFormation, Puppet, Chef, Ansible, or similar) * Experience with CI/CD pipelines, DevOps practices, and Generative AI technologies, including automated deployment, configuration management, continuous integration workflows, prompt engineering, model deployment, and AI\-powered automation tools Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Development Centre Ireland Limited Job ID: A10438710

title: Talent Acquisition Consultant
description: Are you a recruiter looking for your next contract move? We have a number of exciting opportunities just in with a leading global tech brand here in Dublin — and with multiple positions available, we want to hear from you fast! **Role 1 – Early Careers Recruiter \| Dublin City Centre \| 6 Month Contract** Join a world\-class university recruitment programme and manage end\-to\-end hiring for interns, apprentices and graduates. You'll be delivery focused, candidate obsessed, and comfortable working at pace within a global team. **What you need:** * 3\+ years recruitment experience * Strong candidate management and stakeholder skills * Early careers or grad recruitment experience a bonus * Someone who can hit the ground running from day one **Role 2 – Full Cycle Recruiter \| Dublin City Centre \| 6 Month Contract \| x8 Positions** This is a full cycle desk focused on Sales and Customer Success hiring. You'll own the process end to end — sourcing passive talent, managing senior stakeholders, and driving offers to close. 8 positions available so we are moving quickly on this one. **What you need:** * 4\+ years full cycle recruiting experience — agency and/or in\-house * Strong passive sourcing and cold outreach ability * Tech sector experience preferred * Comfortable influencing at senior and executive level * AI fluency and ATS experience an advantage **Both roles offer:** * 📍 Dublin based (Hybrid) * 💰 Salary negotiable * ⏳ Immediate start If this role is of interest to you and you have the desired skills and experience, send your CV to Colette today.

title: Talent Specialist
description: **Your new company** You'll be joining a well‑established organisation with a strong performance‑driven culture that values collaboration, trust, and high standards. The business is known for empowering its people, encouraging continuous improvement, and delivering high‑quality outcomes through strong partnerships and clear values. This is a hybrid role based in Dublin, offering a supportive and professional working environment. **Your new role** As a Talent Specialist on a 6‑month fixed‑term contract, you will play a key role in supporting the full recruitment life cycle across the business. Partnering closely with hiring managers and key stakeholders, you'll deliver effective, timely hiring solutions while ensuring a positive candidate experience throughout.Your responsibilities will include: * Managing end‑to‑end recruitment processes for a variety of roles * Building strong relationships with hiring managers and providing recruitment advice and market insight * Proactively sourcing candidates through direct sourcing methods such as LinkedIn, talent pooling and networking * Developing pipelines for niche and hard‑to‑fill roles * Managing requisitions, compliance and data accuracy within the ATS * Producing regular recruitment activity reports and updates **What you'll need to succeed** To be successful in this role, you will have: * Proven experience in either in‑house or agency recruitment * Strong stakeholder management and influencing skills * Hands‑on experience using an ATS (Workday experience is advantageous) * A track record of successful direct sourcing and pipeline development * Excellent organisational skills and attention to detail * A strong focus on delivering an exceptional candidate experience * Location is best accessed via car. There is no public transport nearby.\*\*\* **What you'll get in return** * A 6‑month FTC with a reputable and well‑structured organisation * Hybrid work available * Exposure to a collaborative HR and recruitment function * The opportunity to add strong, recognisable experience to your CV * A professional, supportive environment with clear processes and expectations. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. title: Technical Sales Engineer
description: **Technical Sales Engineer** Location: Home\-based with regular travel to client sites as required **About the Opportunity** We are partnering with a leading civil engineering company seeking an ambitious Technical Sales Engineer or Sales Manager to spearhead growth across Ireland, particularly within the commercial, infrastructure industries.. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced technical sales professional from the civil engineering sector to join a progressive business recognised for its innovative and sustainable engineering solutions **The Company** Our client is a leading specialist contractor, recognised for delivering innovative and sustainable solutions to some of the most complex ground engineering and structural challenges. Operating across highways, rail, aviation, ports and harbours, utilities, data centres, public infrastructure, dilapidation, and subsidence projects, they have built a reputation for technical excellence and outstanding project delivery. With ambitious growth plans for 2026, 2027, and beyond, this fast\-paced global organisation is continuing to expand its presence in existing and emerging markets. Their commitment to innovation, investment, and environmentally conscious, low\-disruption methodologies has positioned them as a trusted partner to clients worldwide and an exciting business for ambitious professionals looking to advance their careers. **Role Overview** As Technical Sales Engineer, you’ll manage and develop client relationships across Ireland, providing tailored ground engineering solutions to the built environment sectors. You’ll take ownership of the sales cycle from lead qualification to project design, quotation, and closing deals. This is a hands\-on, field\-based role where building trust with homeowners and professional stakeholders is key. **Key Responsibilities:** * Identify and develop new business opportunities in the region * Conduct site assessments and provide expert advice on engineering solutions * Design technical solutions based on survey data * Prepare quotes and follow up to secure orders * Ensure seamless handover to the operations team for project delivery * Provide after\-sales support and resolve any client issues * Maintain accurate records using the CRM system * Meet and exceed sales targets **What We’re Looking For:** * Demonstrable sales experience in technical, engineering, construction, or other relevant sectors across the built environment * A relevant qualification in construction, engineering, or surveying * Excellent communication and relationship\-building skills * Proficiency with virtual tools and CRM systems * Full driving licence valid for use in Ireland * Target\-driven mindset with a proactive approach **Package:** We offer a competitive salary with a commission scheme designed to reward over\-achievement, giving high performers a genuine opportunity to earn £100k\+. Additional benefits include a car allowance, pension, healthcare, and 25 days holiday plus bank holidays. Inbound leads will support your success, but a proactive, self\-driven approach to outbound sales will be key to unlocking your full earning potential. If you’re an ambitious problem solver who thrives on autonomy and building relationships, we’d love to hear from you at Elb. Please don’t hesitate to get in touch about this role or any future sales engineering opportunities

title: Technical Solutions Consultant, Apps and Gaming
description: Google welcomes people with disabilities. Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: **London, UK; Dublin, Ireland; Paris, France** . **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * Experience in technical project management, stakeholder management, professional services, solution engineering, or technical consulting. * Experience in writing and reading code (e.g., Java, C\+\+, Python). * Experience with WebTechnologies (HTML, CSS, and JavaScript). **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience as a developer in the mobile app space (iOS or Android) or an understanding of the mobile ad business generation industry. * Experience working with SQL. * Experience working alongside technical teams (Product, Engineering, or Business) to solve ambiguous problems. * Ability to translate technical concepts into clear, advice for non\-technical stakeholders or external partners. * Excellent problem\-solvoing skills with a track record of debugging customer\-facing issues, whether it's at the code level or within a broader system implementation. **About The Job** As a Technical Solutions Consultant, you will be responsible for the technical relationship of our largest advertising clients and/or product partners. You will lead cross\-functional teams in Engineering, Sales and Product Management to leverage emerging technologies for our external clients/partners. From concept design and testing to data analysis and support, you will oversee the technical execution and business operations of Google's online advertising platforms and/or product partnerships. You will be able to balance business and partner needs with technical constraints, develop innovative, cutting edge solutions and act as a partner and consultant to those you are working with. You will also be able to build tools and automate products, oversee the technical execution and business operations of Google's partnerships, as well as develop product strategy and prioritize projects and resources. Google creates products and services that make the world a better place, and gTech’s role is to help bring them to life. Our teams of trusted advisors support customers globally. Our solutions are rooted in our technical skill, product expertise, and a thorough understanding of our customers’ complex needs. Whether the answer is a bespoke solution to solve a unique problem, or a new tool that can scale across Google, everything we do aims to ensure our customers benefit from the full potential of Google products. To learn more about gTech, check out our video . Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. France: €64000 \- €66000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Cultivate technical and product knowledge of the mobile ad business generation ecosystem to architect solutions and implementations for our partners. * Lead technical engagements with mobile app developers, aligning Google’s product suite with their specific business goals to drive long\-term business growth and engagement. * Resolve technical escalations from business and clients, while proactively developing tools, documentation, and workflows to scale support across the EMEA region. * Identify and implement best practices to streamline business strategies, accelerating product adoption and establishing a blueprint for partner success. * Act as a critical feedback loop between the market and the product to influence strategy, design, and prioritization in collaboration with Go\-to\-Market, Product Management, and Engineering teams. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Technical Support Specialist
description: Sim Local is a leading global travel eSIM provider, helping millions of travellers stay connected in over 200 destinations worldwide. Through a combination of local operator partnerships, innovative digital products, and omnichannel distribution, we deliver seamless connectivity experiences for international travellers. We are looking for a motivated individual to join the team as a Technical Support Specialist. This is a hands\-on role covering internal IT operations, B2B client\-facing support, incident response, and platform tooling. We are open to applications from recent graduates or candidates with up to 2 years of experience. The right attitude and communication skills matter most — full training and onboarding will be provided. This is a Dublin\-based hybrid role. Office access is required periodically to manage hardware, servers, and on\-site equipment. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Dundrum, County Dublin

title: Technician, re:Cycle Reverse Logistics, re:Cycle Reverse Logistics
description: **Description** re:Cycle Reverse Logistics is an electronics processing, reuse, and recycling company that works to extend the life of used electronic equipment through reuse, repair, and recycling. We are a stand\-alone company within the Amazon corporate family that provides services to other Amazon companies. We specialize in the handling of valuable electronic equipment that was originally used in Data Centers. re:Cycle Reverse Logistics is looking for Technicians for its Reverse Logistics operation based in Dublin, Ireland. Our mission is to manage and scale information technology asset disposition (ITAD) services for used electronics and electronic equipment (UEEE) through secure and environmentally sustainable practices supporting asset recovery, reuse, recycling, and other circular economy initiatives. Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Key job responsibilities * Failure Analysis \- Conduct a range of Testing, Repair, Sanitization and Disassembly across a variety of Data Center IT Server and Networking Equipment \& Components. * Warehouse Management System \- Shipping \& Receiving parts, maintain inventory accuracy, carry out audits, and disposition parts as prescribed by Inventory/Warehouse Management System. * Unload, Pick \& Pack orders/parts for shipment across local, EU and non\-EU sites. * Inventory Management – maintain precise records of all commodities – inbound and outbound. **About The Team** Diverse Experiences Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee\-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship and Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. Willing and able to frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach. **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of computer hardware components and operating systems * Knowledge of computer hardware components and operating systems. Proficient with computers and Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Excel). * Understanding of shipping \& receiving, inventory management warehousing practices. * Reach Truck License. Commitment to a culture of Safety \& Security. * Knowledge of Inventory Management Systems. Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. title: Technology Graduate
description: Assiduous is a fast\-growing Irish headquartered fintech startup revolutionising how small and medium\-sized enterprises (SMEs) access corporate finance expertise. Our flagship platform, the Corporate Finance Autopilot®, is an AI\-native solution that enables business owners to plan, forecast, and execute their most valuable strategic and financial transactions. We’re at an exciting stage of growth and are seeking an exceptional technology graduate to support the development of our software solution. This role is open to class of 2026 graduates with a keen interest in AI native platforms and FinTech, with a degree in computer science or a related discipline. We are recruiting a Technology Graduate with a strong interest in the application of computer science, modern AI tools and cloud technologies to the high use case of corporate finance. This is a rare opportunity to join an ambitious Irish fintech at a formative stage, where you will be instrumental in the development of the Corporate Finance Autopilot®, collaborating directly with other AI Product Engineers, product owners, design partners and early adopter customers. You will be expected to contribute immediately to writing and shipping production ready code, with assistance from the latest AI coding tools and demonstrate a strong understanding and interest in the subject matter of corporate finance.

We are currently seeking an experienced Teleporter Operator to support site\-based operations on key client projects in Ireland. **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate and conditions * Long\-term project work with a trusted, well\-established contractor * Opportunity to work across innovative, fast\-paced industrial projects **Key Responsibilities** * Operate the teleporter in a safe and efficient manner across a live construction or installation site * Assist with the transport, movement, and positioning of materials, equipment, and components * Conduct daily machine checks and report any mechanical issues promptly * Work alongside trades, supervisors, and safety personnel to maintain smooth site logistics * Support other general site duties when machine operation is not required * Adhere to all site\-specific safety regulations and procedures **Required Qualifications \& Skills** * Valid Teleporter ticket/certification required * Safe Pass required * Manual Handling certification preferred * Strong experience on industrial or high\-tech construction sites * Strong safety awareness and good communication on busy, technical sites **Contact:** hr@ross\-es.com Job Type: Full\-time Ability to commute/relocate: * Ratoath, County Meath: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Licence/Certification: * teleporter ticket/certification (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

On the occasions where we have high volumes of applicants, some roles may close earlier than the advertised end date in order for us to manage all of the applicants appropriately. We will only be able to offer individual feedback to those candidates who attend an interview. For more information about us please visit www.tescoplc.com Â

title: Total Spare Management Administrator
description: **About the Company** MSR\-FSR is an international managed engineering services provider supporting leading organisations in the high\-technology sector. We collaborate with major global clients and are recognised for delivering high\-quality engineering solutions. We offer structured training, career development opportunities, and the chance to work on large\-scale projects across Europe. **Position Overview** We are currently recruiting for a **Total Spare Management Administrator**. This role (after training period is complete) will be a hybrid role of **3 days onsite and 2 days WFH, at Manager discretion.** The Total Spare Management Administrator manages and controls semiconductor manufacturing spares across plants/production lines to ensure equipment uptime. This role focuses on accurate spare part master data, inventory control, spare request fulfilment, stock audits, and readiness planning aligned with semiconductor manufacturing requirements. **Key Responsibilities** * Spare parts management system\*\* (spare module/ERP/CMMS integration), ensuring accurate item setup and lifecycle status. * Maintain complete and correct \*\*critical spares master data\*\*: * Part numbers, alternates, BOM/kit definitions (if used), manufacturer references, compatibility, and substitutes * Lead times, MOQ, storage requirements, and serialisation/traceability rules (if applicable) \- Manage the full spare flow: * Receiving/put\-away confirmation \- inspection/quality checks coordination (as required). * Issuance to maintenance/production teams based on approvals and procedures \- returns, scrap/write\-off processing, and root\-cause support for discrepancies. * Monitor \*\*stock health\*\* for critical equipment: \- min/max levels, reorder points, safety stock, and buffer planning. * Ensure correct part selection for equipment models/lines \- ensure timely fulfilment to support downtime reduction targets. * Conduct and support \*\*cycle counts, audits, and reconciliations\*\*: * Investigate variances and ensure corrective actions are recorded \- Support uptime strategy by identifying and maintaining: * \*\*A/B/C critical spares\*\* classification (or company\-defined criticality tiers) \- consumption trends, slow\-moving/excess stock reporting * Track and report \*\*spare performance metrics\*\* to leadership: \- stock availability/service level, stock\-out causes, turnaround time for requests * Overdue orders, supplier performance inputs (lead time adherence) * Work closely with \*\*Procurement/Supply Chain\*\*: \- trigger replenishment actions * Manage vendor documentation and ensure part data alignment with purchasing * Ensure compliance with semiconductor\-specific requirements. * ESD\-safe handling/storage rules, cleanliness/packing standards when applicable. * Traceability and documentation retention for regulated/critical components. * Maintain controlled access and governance: \- standard approval workflows. * Change management for master data updates. **Requirements*** Diploma/Degree in \*\*Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, or Business\*\* (or equivalent experience). * Proven experience in \*\*spares/inventory administration\*\* and/or equipment maintenance support. * Hands\-on experience with \*\*ERP/CMMS or inventory systems\*\* (SAP, Oracle, Maximo, etc.). * Strong data accuracy and reconciliation skills. * Intermediate\-to\-advanced \*\*Excel\*\* (reports, pivot tables, inventory analytics). * Experience in \*\*semiconductor manufacturing\*\* or high\-tech maintenance environments. * Familiarity with \*\*equipment spare criticality, lead\-time planning, and reliability\-driven inventory\*\*. * Knowledge of maintenance/production downtime processes (CMMS workflows). \#\#\# Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) * Inventory accuracy (audit variance %) \- Spare request cycle time (request\-to\-issue) \- Stock\-out frequency and severity (critical spares) * Reorder compliance (on\-time replenishment) \- Reduction in excess/obsolete spares while maintaining availability. **Benefits** **What We Offer** * Comprehensive technical training program. * Opportunities for career growth within an international environment. * Exposure to advanced semiconductor technologies. * A professional and supportive team.

title: Trainee Actuary/Pricing Analyst - 12 Month FTC
description: Job Description: **Description** We are currently recruiting a Trainee Actuary/Pricing Analyst for a 12 Month FTC in our Pricing \& Underwriting Department. Reporting to the Commercial Lines Pricing Manager, your role will be to support the pricing function within AXA Ireland to achieve overall financial targets for all classes of business / channels / territories written by AXA Ireland. At AXA we work smart, empowering our people to balance their time between home and the office in a way that works best for them, their team, and our customers. You'll spend the majority of your working week away from home, either at one of our office locations, visiting clients or attending industry events. You will work in a team of analysts and actuaries: **your main responsibilities will include:** * Preparing data, including extracting target data from multiple databases, integrating datasets, creating derived variables, applying business rules, and performing quality control checks to ensure data accuracy and reliability. * Exploring datasets (internal/external) which can help price and underwrite risks more effectively * Developing rate change proposals and supporting impact analyses to maximise portfolio value. * Ensuring that pricing actions are aligned to overall AXA account targets and strategic financial plan. * Abiding by AXA customer fairness expectations and all applicable regulations. * Making pricing recommendations to ensure profitability and communicating these effectively to the business. * Monitoring performance of rating actions and overall account KPIs against expectations and identifying solutions as required. * Providing support on requests for additional pricing analysis when required. * Deriving and sharing account optimisation insights and analysis at regular account review meetings. * Working collaboratively within the Pricing \& Underwriting team and sharing best practices. * Maintaining existing internal tools by implementing new features and addressing any issues or bugs to ensure continuous improvement and reliability. * Representing Pricing team on cross\-functional projects within AXA Ireland. * Demonstrating flexibility within the role, being prepared to undertake other duties as assigned by line manager from time to time. **About You:** * Candidates must hold a numerical degree in Statistics, Actuarial Studies, Engineering, Mathematics, or Computer Science. A master’s degree in these areas is advantageous but not mandatory. * Ideally, candidates will have 2\+ years of experience in a similar Trainee Actuary, Pricing Analyst, or Data Science role. * Experience working in Commercial Lines pricing desirable including understanding the insurance industry and external influences. * Proven experience using SAS, R and/or other programming languages. * Strong knowledge of data sources, analytical tools, and key business drivers. * Ability to identify and communicate limitations of tools and methods. * Proven experience using Microsoft Office suite. * Detailed\-oriented, problem\-solver and solution focused with proven ability to deliver, prioritising and organising workload. * Autonomous, innovative, pragmatic, and capable of applying both known and new techniques. * Ability to work on multiple projects at the same time. * Able to apply analytical problem solving, make recommendations, and implement improvements continuously in a dynamic environment. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with an ability to work collaboratively with others Awareness of compliance requirements related to Health \& Safety, IT Security, and relevant legislation within the Pricing \& Underwriting department. * This recruitment campaign will be used to support permanent, temporary and/or extension for any upcoming similar positions.

title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: ContractTypeAndDuration Full Time, Permanent **The role** ------------ **Specsavers, Blanchardstown \- Ireland** Trainee Optical Assistant So, you’re a proactive people person, passionate about retail and offering the best customer service? Sounds like you’d be a great fit here. So, if you’ve ever considered a career in optics, this Trainee Optical Assistant role could be perfect for you. As a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t need to have previous experience, you just need to be prepared to create an outstanding store environment for both our customers and our team, making sure every patient receives the best care in our stores. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So, whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in the Blanchardstown Shopping Centre, our store has off\-site parking and bike lockups available. With sustainability at the forefront, Specsavers Blanchardstown is a community driven store that actively participates with charities and community initiatives alike. **What’s on Offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * You can expect to earn up to €35,000 once passed probation and relevant learning \& development modules completed * 30 days annual leave (Includes Bank Holidays) * Full time * Professional fees paid * Specsavers Perks – our discounted benefits scheme * Annual subscription to the “Headspace” app * Outstanding clinical and professional development opportunities * Support with IP, other higher accreditations and Pathway * Access to latest clinical technology and equipment * Support with CPD points * Regular team events and team building evenings **What we’re looking for?** If this is still sounding like a bit of you, we do need you to have a few skills to get started as a Trainee Optical Assistant. These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. **This includes** * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. Please be aware you may be requsted to complete a short video as part of the interview process! **Checked all the boxes? title: Trainee Optical Assistant

These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. **This includes** * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. title: Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Veterinary Nurse \- Small Animal** ==================================== ### ***Ark Vetcare, Foxrock, Co Dublin*** ### ***Full\-time, Permanent \\u007C From August 2026*** ### ***Salary commensurate with experience*** **Join a Supportive and Experienced Team** ------------------------------------------ Ark Vetcare Foxrock is seeking a Veterinary Nurse to join our busy and well\-established small animal practice. This is an excellent opportunity for nurses at all stages of their career, including newly qualified graduates, with strong support and mentorship available from our experienced team. We are proud of our collaborative environment, where nurses are encouraged to learn, develop, and fully engage in all aspects of patient care while working alongside skilled veterinary surgeons and support staff. **The Role** ------------ As a Veterinary Nurse within our primary care team, you will play a key role in delivering high\-quality clinical care and ensuring the wellbeing of patients throughout their journey in the practice. Your responsibilities will include supporting consultations and surgical procedures, monitoring patient recovery, administering treatments, maintaining clinical areas, and providing guidance to pet owners. You will also contribute to maintaining accurate records and upholding high clinical and professional standards. This is a hands\-on, varied role suited to someone who is proactive, compassionate, and enthusiastic about developing their nursing skills in a busy GP setting. **Working Hours** ----------------- This is a full\-time role of 37\.5 hours per week, worked across a structured rota. Shifts will typically fall between 7:30am and 3:30pm or 11:00am and 7:00pm, Monday to Friday, with a 30\-minute break each day. The rota includes 1 in 4 Saturdays (8:00am to 3:00pm). There are no Sunday or out\-of\-hours requirements, and only one bank holiday per year is required, supporting a strong work\-life balance. **Our Practice** ---------------- Ark Vetcare Foxrock is a busy, first\-opinion practice equipped with modern facilities including a dedicated operating theatre, dental suite, large in\-house laboratory, digital x\-ray, ultrasound, and separate dog and cat kennelling. Our team consists of experienced Veterinary Surgeons, skilled Veterinary Nurses, and a supportive administrative team. We work in a positive, team\-oriented environment where everyone contributes, supports one another, and takes pride in delivering excellent patient and client care. As part of the wider Ark Vetcare group, we also benefit from the support of our branch practices in Dun Laoghaire and Kilmacud. **What We Offer** ----------------- We are committed to supporting our people and creating a workplace where you can thrive. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including generous annual leave, wellbeing support, paid professional memberships, and structured development opportunities. You will have access to: ### **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave ### **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave * Company sick pay ### **Development** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities ### **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid ### **Additional benefits** * Annual pay reviews * Pawesome benefits – various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Free flu jabs * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) **Why Join Us** --------------- At Ark Vetcare, we combine high clinical standards with a genuinely supportive and enjoyable working culture. We work hard, but we also value teamwork, individuality, and creating a positive environment where everyone feels part of the team. Whether you are just starting your career or looking to build on your experience, this is a role where you will be supported to grow and make a real impact. Salary DOE We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Work based learning Tusla Training School - Special Care Services
description: The Child and Family Agency was established on 1st January 2014 and is responsible for a range of statutory functions including provision of child protection, alternative care, specified regulatory services and a range of family support services. The Agency has commenced a major improvement programme with significant focus on Practice, Culture and Structure. The Agency currently has responsibility for a budget in excess of €1\.02bn and delivers its services through circa 5,000 people in 350 locations across the Country.The Child and Family Agency has responsibility for the following range of services:Child Protection and Welfare Family Support Alternative Care Adoption Tusla Education Support Services (TESS)Children’s Service Regulation Counselling and Therapeutic Supports Further information is available on www.tusla.ie *Applications are invited from Social Care qualified candidates or those who hold qualification in:* * *Youth and Community Work* * *BA (Hons) Youth and Community Development* * *BA (Hons) Psychology* * *Addiction Studies (Level 7\)* * *BA (Hons) Counselling and Addiction Studies* * *Disability Diploma (Primary Care)* * *Degree in Social Science (minimum Level 7\)* Purpose of role: This programme involves 3 months classroom\-based learning followed by 3 months’ work placement in Tusla Special Care Unit in Dublin.The purpose of the post is to provide the employee with on the job learning opportunities as a work\-based learner in social care with access to shadow a social care worker, learn social care skills while providing direct administration support to the social care services that seeks to improve the health and social wellbeing of the service users within the context of local communities and children’s residential and special care services. On successful completion of the 6\-month programme and evidence of competency assessment, you will be offered a permanent contract of employment. During the Work Based Learning Programme, you will be paid a salary of €37,752 (pro rata). Applications are invited by CV, together with a personal statement clearly stating suitability for the Work Based Learning Programme to socialcaregraduate@tusla.ie **.**

title: Workday Business Analyst
description: **We are open to this being fixed\-term employment or day rate contract. The project is for 6 months.** **About the role** The Business Analyst role will support optimisation of our Workday platform and delivery of Workday's Advanced Compensation module through improved processes, configuration, and governance across HR, Reward, and technical teams. This is a 6\-month contract reporting into our HR Master Data Lead. The role can be performed remotely or in one of our office locations (hybrid options available). **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting Workday optimisation initiatives, identifying opportunities to streamline functionality and improve user experience. * Facilitating workshops, process mapping, and requirements gathering with HR, Reward and business stakeholders. * Translating business requirements into Workday functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria. * Implementing automated solutions that resolve or reduce manual processes and align with Workday best practice. * Supporting the creation, embedding and maintenance of HR data governance, including data definitions, controls, data quality frameworks and change governance. * Developing and maintaining process documentation, including process flows, SOPs, configuration guides and change logs. * Supporting UAT planning and execution, including test script creation and defect management. **About you** * Proven hands‑on experience with Workday HCM, including configuration, tenant review and troubleshooting. * Experience implementing or enhancing Workday Advanced Compensation module (merit, bonus, stock). * Strong capability in process improvement and understanding end‑to‑end HR process flows. * Skilled at working with technical teams to ensure accurate configuration, integration and reporting outcomes. * Understanding of HR processes, including Core HR, Talent, Compensation and data governance. * Ability to translate business needs into clear functional specifications. * Strong documentation, analytical and problem‑solving skills. **What Success Looks Like** * Optimised Workday processes that deliver a smoother, more intuitive experience for HR and the business. * Well defined, clearly articulated requirements and documentation that support high‑quality system changes. * A successful rollout of Advanced Compensation, with engaged stakeholders and minimal defects. * Strong governance in place, with clear data definitions, quality controls and change processes. * HR teams confident in new processes and functionality due to effective training and knowledge transfer. * Continuous improvement embedded, with feedback loops that drive ongoing Workday enhancements. **About Inizio** At Inizio, we're a global team driven by a shared purpose: to reimagine health. Formed in 2022 through the coming together of Ashfield and Huntsworth, we unite decades of experience with cutting\-edge capabilities to deliver impactful solutions across the commercialization journey \- from discovery to post\-launch. Our global network of over 10,000 experts operates across 50 countries, supporting clients with strategic insight, medical excellence, creative engagement, and real\-world delivery. We work with the world's leading health and life sciences businesses to solve complex challenges and drive meaningful outcomes for our clients and patients. What sets Inizio apart is our people \- and we believe everyone should have the opportunity to thrive. Here, you're encouraged to reimagine your possibilities: to grow, to be curious and to make real change happen. It's a place where your individuality is valued, your development is supported, and your ambition is celebrated. If you're ready to push boundaries and accelerate your career, Inizio is the best place to be. **Our Pledge** At Inizio, we value inclusivity, recognize the power of diversity and inspire the next generation of change\-makers. We are an equal opportunities employer. We believe in creating a dynamic work environment that values diversity, equity and inclusion. We aim to recruit from a diverse slate of candidates and foster an environment that provides the right conditions for long\-term success. We welcome all applications regardless of race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity, age, national origin, marital status, military veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation and physical or mental disability. *Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.*

title: kitchen team member
description: **kitchen team member \|** full time three whole decades of acting different, we don’t conform, we celebrate difference. **appreciating personality over perfection – we call it be you, be wagamama.** whether you’re looking to start a **career in the kitchen** or just looking for a **wok inspired side hustle** this could be the role for you. with over 165 restaurants and growing in the uk \+ ireland, we’re on a mission to nourish us all from bowl to soul **the role \|** as a **kitchen team member** at wagamama you'll play a key role in our kitchen, preparing and serving our **fresh food** to standard with care. working alongside a **talented team** of over 100 nationalities, not only will you gain **key life skills** at wagamama, you'll make great friends too, all whilst ensuring that our guests receive delicious food the wagamama way **if you...** * would love to provide exceptional dishes for our guests * have a genuine passion for fresh food, we don’t use microwaves or heat lamps in our kitchens * like buzzing environments, keeping organised and have excellent attention to detail * foster a positive, inclusive work environment and empower others to thrive through training and personal growth opportunities **we’d love to hear from you** **wok’s in it for you \|** * bi\-weekly pay \+ share of the tips * flexible hours to support your side hustle * free food on shift – yes anything from our menu! plus 50% outside of work to treat your friends \+ family * fresh clean uniform provided by us for each shift * be the first to experience our brand\-new menus * wagamama parties throughout the year with vip guests * be known nationwide as a wok star – our recognition programme in addition to this, we offer the usual such as, pension scheme, holiday, training, cycle to work scheme, retail \+ leisure discounts, discounted gym memberships \+ long service awards **be you be wagamama** * 100% of tips go to our teams. actual tips by restaurant will vary. tips are left solely at our guests discretion and are not considered as wages by the company nor are they guaranteed in any way
